programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The arapaima is the worlds largest scaled freshwater fish and considered the ultimate jungle catch. The crew heads to Ecuador to land one of these living dinosaurs

programme.title: Catfishin Kings
programme.desc: Following expert hand-fishers as they compete in a cross-state catfish-grabbing tournament, hosted by Brady Knowlton

programme.title: Expedition Bigfoot
programme.desc: With just three days left in the Kentucky expedition, the team investigates a strange tree structure that could be key to proving the existence of Bigfoot

programme.title: Lair Of The Sawfish
programme.desc: Experts unravel the mystery behind one of the oceans strangest creatures, the sawfish, from its evolution to its role in the marine ecosystem

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Tom tries to conquer his fear of rodents, Cameron battles his hatred of birds, Jackie addresses her dog phobia and Ramona deals with her terror around snakes

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Alan and Shannon wanted a cat to cuddle, but instead they got Barnabas - a hissing, flesh-ripping feline who can only be calmed by opera music

programme.title: Must Love Cats
programme.desc: John visits the students at Kitty Kindergarten, investigates the latest smartphone apps for high-tech felines, and discovers how cats can survive falls from great heights

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Wardens question a group of hunters suspected of trespassing and theft. Plus, lifeless ducks are found floating mysteriously in Port Aransas

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff tries to mend a German shepherds broken elbow for its war veteran owner, and a cat with a swollen bladder needs difficult surgery to save its life

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff tries to save a dog kicked in the head by a horse, and tackles a neighbourhood overrun with feral cats. A pet ferret displays worrying symptoms

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Before heading to New York Citys Dog Bowl, Mariah and M2 prepare their most eligible rescues for the big day. Tony and Earl discover a wounded stray

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Moe and Lizzy reveal they are expecting a child and the family band together to host a gender reveal party. Lizzy goes the extra mile for her favourite dog

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: A baby hippo arrives and an orphaned koala needs support. A team travel to Kenya to share their innovative milk formula for elephant calves

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson sets out to create a treehouse bed-and-breakfast designed for romance, featuring stained glass, a bedroom and the biggest bathroom Pete has ever built

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: Two rehabilitated sloths are released back into the wild, the team reveals the athletic secrets of the jungle gym, and the resident scientist inspects a sloth skeleton

programme.title: Meet the Sloths
programme.desc: A tracking system is tested by sanctuary favourite Sidwiggy, the nursery manager becomes an anger management counsellor, and the scientist discusses sloth fur

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: More secrets from the animal kingdom, including why moths are attracted to flames, why turtles cry and why sea otters bond with stones

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?
programme.desc: Experts spill the secrets of the animal kingdom, revealing why spiders dont get stuck in their own webs and how foxes find water in the desert

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.sub-title: In the Heat of the Moment
programme.desc: James Giuliani enlists some help to prepare his animals for an adopt-a-thon. Plus, he must rely on his boys brute force to release a dog from a locked car

programme.title: The Diamond Collar
programme.sub-title: No More Room at the Inn
programme.desc: Dr Ps kennels reach maximum capacity, and he gives James Giuliani three days to move all his rescues out. The team vow to respond by finding a place of their own

programme.title: Alaska Gold Diggers
programme.desc: SJ and her daughters pool their savings and head to Nome, Alaska, in an effort to reopen the family gold mines and keep their legacy alive

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Brett Raymer and Wayde King are asked to create their biggest-ever Christmas-themed tank. Plus, a Snowglobe product is requested for a winter celebration

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Six of the companys employees - Brett, Wayde, Heather, Agnes, Redneck and the General - take a trip down memory lane by each choosing their favourite tanks built by ATM

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys visit a homestead in Oregon where fire is a constant threat and the hungry family see their livestock more like family pets than food

programme.title: Great Hammerhead Invasion
programme.desc: Leading shark scientist Tristan Guttridge tries to find out why, every November, the usually solitary giant hammerhead sharks congregate off the coast of Florida

programme.title: Super Predator
programme.desc: Film-maker Dave Riggs embarks on a quest to determine what creature managed to savagely devour a nine-foot-long great white shark by donning a one-man underwater exosuit and venturing over 1,000 feet to the bottom of Bremers Canyon, off Western Australia

programme.title: Swarm Chasers
programme.desc: Part one of two. Every 50 years villagers in Bangladesh must prepare for a surge of black rats. Rodent expert Dr Steven Belmain attempts to solve the mystery

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Marco and Aimee recently got married, but Marcos cat Monty is tearing them apart with his appetite for destruction - and will eat everything in sight

programme.title: Preposterous Pets
programme.desc: The story of an obsessive fan of lizards, who changed his name to Henry Lizardlover and found fame by taking photos of his scaly friends posing as humans

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Wayde and Brett try to come up with a solution to stop the acrylic from scratching off a skateboard ramp tank. Meanwhile, the ATM team builds an octopus tank for Waydes birthday

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Charles Jantzen leads the biggest horse rescue of the year, while investigator Trischa Price comes to the aid of a dog left at home alone

programme.title: Animal Cops Houston
programme.desc: Charles Jantzen leads an investigation into a kennel suspected of housing more than 50 ill-treated dogs

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys visit a homestead in Oregon where fire is a constant threat and the hungry family see their livestock more like family pets than food

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia rescues a neglected puppy living in filthy conditions. Two owners want a companion for their four-year-old dog, Chase

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: A pair of rescued mountain lion cubs are ready for their new habitat. Plus, a white rhino undergoes an intense procedure in the hope of saving the species!

programme.title: The Zoo: San Diego
programme.desc: Hyena brothers are moved into the lions habitat. Plus, the staff are ecstatic when a zebra shows signs of pregnancy and Mabel the pygmy hippo welcomes a calf

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Oyster season keeps the wardens busy on the land and on water. Hunters are investigated for trespassing and a deck hand with several warrants is questioned

programme.title: Dr Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Amy meets Bobo the cat, who is badly wounded after a fight, and staff from animal control bring in neglected dog Juicy

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson seeks out the most wonderful arboreal structures in the world, showing how each has been influenced by different artistic movements

programme.title: Great Hammerhead Invasion
programme.desc: Leading shark scientist Tristan Guttridge tries to find out why, every November, the usually solitary giant hammerhead sharks congregate off the coast of Florida

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: The Kilchers prepare for another season in the wild, but their way of life faces a bigger threat than ever before when a massive earthquake strikes the region

programme.title: Edge of Alaska
programme.desc: Neil defies both nature and the wishes of the locals by developing his mine tour, and the discovery of his secret construction project causes further antagonism

programme.title: Super Predator
programme.desc: Film-maker Dave Riggs embarks on a quest to determine what creature managed to savagely devour a nine-foot-long great white shark by donning a one-man underwater exosuit and venturing over 1,000 feet to the bottom of Bremers Canyon, off Western Australia

programme.title: Alaska: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: The Raneys come to the aid of a North Carolina couple whose cabin is so ramshackle they have exposed wires in the shower and their livestock has been half-eaten by predators

programme.title: My Extreme Animal Phobia
programme.desc: Phobia expert Dr Robin Zasio comes to the aid of Katie, who is afraid of spiders and will go out of her way to secure every nook and cranny against them. The programme also features Andrews fear of cockroaches and Jackies fear of butterflies

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete Nelson installs a stainless steel kitchen in the trees for a family of sweet-makers. The treehouse also boasts barrel ceilings and a loft bedroom

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Twelve investigators have police back-up on a dangerous assignment to arrest a dog breeder selling sick puppies

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: Officers from the Pennsylvania SPCA respond to a call concerning abandoned animals, but are unprepared for what they discover inside a fridge

programme.title: Gershwins Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heywards song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Why the Industrial Revolution Happened Here
programme.desc: Jeremy Black examines one of the most extraordinary periods in English history, explaining the unique economic, social and political conditions that led to the nation becoming the richest and most powerful on Earth by the 19th century. He traces the unprecedented explosion of new ideas and technological inventions that transformed an agrarian society into an increasingly industrial and urbanised one

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The Peoples Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie OSullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day nine at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring the second and penultimate sessions of two second-round matches. Should they have both won their first-round encounters, Ronnie OSullivan and John Higgins will continue their long-standing rivalry this evening. The two players have won this competition 11 times between them, with seven of those titles going to OSullivan. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Orchestral concert film, taking a journey along rivers around the world accompanied by music composed by Richard Tognetti and William Barton. Footage from different landscapes reveal how rivers have shaped the world, creating caverns, valleys and fertile plains, and revealing how they have shaped the history of human civilisation, and been affected by it in turn. Narrated by Willem Dafoe

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Rivieras artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote dAzurs beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The splintering that occurred under the Stuart kings, as political, religious and cultural divisions fuelled war across three kingdoms. Architect Amanda Levete climbs the Tulip Stairs in the Queens House, Greenwich, and reassesses Inigo Jones elegant and innovative design, while portrait artist Tai Shan Schierenberg encounters Van Dycks monumental portrait of the Earl of Pembrokes family, and finds signs of the dysfunction and tensions which point to the civil war to come

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes turns her attention to Socrates, detailing how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant concludes his exploration of the French Rivieras artistic legacy by focusing on how art and celebrity became intertwined in the early part of the 20th century. Resident artists such as Picasso and Matisse found the sun-drenched landscape ideal for experimenting with their work, and as the region became a creative hub, the likes of novelist Jean Cocteau and ballet impresario Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. This artistic community also liked to party on the Cote dAzurs beaches - a pastime that proved equally irresistible to the American multimillionaires arriving in the area on board transatlantic liners

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer and explorer Paul Rose heads for Wensleydale as he sets out to explore the region, starting in the small town of Hawes, where locals now run many of their own services, including the buses. Paul checks out why cycling is so popular in the Dales and tries to get to the top of the Buttertubs Pass. Along the way he meets a family of dormice and spends time with a man who looks after one of the areas most imposing castles

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming battles his way through blanket weed as he travels along the Shropshire Union Canal and discovers industrial secrets in Audlem, Cheshire

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.sub-title: The Vanished Capital of the Pharaoh
programme.desc: The vanished ancient Egyptian capital Piramesse, constructed 3,000 years ago at the command of pharaoh Rameses the Great. The city sparked confusion when it was found in a location where it could not possibly have been built. Archaeologists Manfred Bietak and Edgar Pusch try to solve the mystery. Narrated by Mark Halliley

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: British Art at War: Bomberg, Sickert and Nash
programme.sub-title: Paul Nash - The Ghosts of War
programme.desc: Presenter Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how three British artists - David Bomberg, Walter Sickert and Paul Nash - responded to the cataclysm of the First World War. He begins by focusing on the work of Paul Nash, who sketched the battlefields of Flanders near Ypres on 25th May 1917. He was so fixed on his work that he tripped and fell into a trench, breaking his ribs - an accident that went on to save his life and influence his later work

programme.title: An Art Lovers Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover the art and architecture of Barcelona, seeking out the Romanesque frescoes that inspired a young Miro and the designs of an engineer who arguably put more of a stamp on the city than its star architect, Antoni Gaudí

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary examining the influence that the jet stream - a ribbon of fast-moving air high up in the atmosphere - has on everyday weather. Here, scientists explain the effects the phenomenon has had on climate and discuss what could happen should it change its pattern of behaviour

programme.title: The 1951 Festival of Britain: Brave New World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how the 1951 Festival of Britain helped the nation to look forward to a brighter future during a period of post-war austerity. Archive footage and personal testimonies reveal how, despite widespread debt and rationing, the event encouraged people to have fun through design and ingenuity

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: The splintering that occurred under the Stuart kings, as political, religious and cultural divisions fuelled war across three kingdoms. Architect Amanda Levete climbs the Tulip Stairs in the Queens House, Greenwich, and reassesses Inigo Jones elegant and innovative design, while portrait artist Tai Shan Schierenberg encounters Van Dycks monumental portrait of the Earl of Pembrokes family, and finds signs of the dysfunction and tensions which point to the civil war to come

programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Action from Manchester City v Southampton at Wembley Stadium in the semi-finals of the FA Cup, and Premier League matches, including Arsenal v Newcastle United at Emirates Stadium. The Saints were looking for a second successive major scalp in the competition after overcoming Arsenal in the previous round. Meanwhile, the Gunners continued their title challenge against an inconsistent Newcastle side. Plus, Fulham v Aston Villa, Liverpool v Crystal Palace, West Ham United v Everton and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Tottenham Hotspur

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents live coverage of the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, as approximately 59,000 competitors of all abilities take to the streets of the capital. Ethiopias Tigst Assefa and Sabastian Sawe of Kenya won the respective womens and mens elite races last year and the wheelchair races were won by Swiss athletes Marcel Hug and Catherine Debrunner. With commentary by Andrew Cotter, Steve Cram, Paula Radcliffe and Tanni Grey-Thompson, and reports from JJ Chalmers, Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: A look ahead to the latest forecast

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers talks to Sinn Fein president Mary Lou McDonald after her partys Ard Fheis in Belfast. Professor Jon Tonge from the University of Liverpool assesses Sinn Feins electoral chances

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Music and worship from around the country

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Appeal on behalf of Action for ME, a charity that provides support and advocacy to people affected by ME, while looking to create positive change through medical research

programme.title: Despicable Me 2 (2013)
programme.desc: A reformed criminal mastermind who has abandoned his wicked ways for a life of fatherhood and jam-making helps a secret agent to bring one of his fellow evil geniuses to justice. The villain in question has stolen a dangerous experimental serum with the power to turn harmless animals into ravening monsters. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Steve Carell and Kristen Wiig

programme.title: Trace, Track, Get My Car Back
programme.desc: Tracker Shaquille is desperate to hunt down a car before its smuggled out of the country by an eastern European gang of car thieves and shipped to Romania. Meanwhile, in Guildford, tracker Richard is searching for a digger thats been hidden on the edge of a golf course

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Vale of Evesham: Bridging the Hungry Gap
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild micro-climate have long made it one of Britains most productive growing regions, with a long history of producing food even in leaner months. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the animals that have made a home in a woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley. A tawny owl hunts for rodents drawn to the garden bird feeders, while a dormouse hibernates beneath the fallen leaves. In spring, a queen buff-tailed bumblebee emerges from a winter underground and prepares to build a new colony and a badger mother brings her young above ground for the first time

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Beaumaris Castle on the island of Anglesey, where two antiques reveal how Welsh culture and language have been both suppressed and celebrated over the years. Other finds include a collection of carved wooden dogs and two pieces of Faberge jewellery which would have graced the highest levels of the pre-revolutionary Russian aristocracy

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Crime drama, starring Sheridan Smith and Michael Socha as two casino employees who catch each other trying to steal from their employer. They agree to both abandon their plans, but as their lives spiral out of control, the pact theyve made frays, setting them on a high-stakes crash course with gangsters, police, and each other

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leeds United. Action from the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium . The Blues thrashed Port Vale 7-0 in the quarter-finals and are looking to lift the trophy for the first time since 2018. Leeds only triumph in this competition was back in 1972 and they defeated West Ham United on penalties in the previous round

programme.title: The Womens Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekends games in the Super League, including Everton v Chelsea, Brighton and Hove Albion v Manchester City and Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester United

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia presents highlights from her first series, featuring appearances from Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler and Jamie Dornan

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical discussion and expert advice for viewers

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Home Improvement Company Lies Cost Me Thousands
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the case of the homeowner who paid a company nearly £2,000 up-front for garden maintenance, but has nothing to show for it. In the first of a new series of How-To Guides, consumer expert Harry Kind explains how to find a tradesperson you can trust

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin looks at a down-at-heel 1930s Nottingham bungalow thats been transformed into a modern, stylish house. Martel Maxwell and Jacqui Joseph report on developers in Workington, in Cumbria, and Gillingham, in Kent, as they work wonders on their auction buys

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Gary Pe assist the teams at an antiques centre near Exeter, and Christina Trevanion meets a museum curator with a collection of shipwrecked Lego pieces

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Chepstow, friends Ella and Issy race to turn charity shop finds into profit, taking bold risks on furniture and fashion before a tense live-streamed auction

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Interior designer Banjo Beale and his team are brought in to help two small businesses who survive by selling their produce via honesty boxes. Mary runs a small sandwich shop on Mull and is too busy with family matters to open the shop all day and wants to make her honesty box stand out and attract visitors. In South Uist, in the Outer Hebrides, Donna currently runs the honesty box for her ice-cream from an old livestock trailer and wants Banjo to build something more imaginative and fun

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a family from the coastal city of Southend-on-Sea find a Suffolk home with a holiday let, then stops by a boatyard which restores historic boats

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Firefighter Lisa wants to become a signwriter in the Scottish Highlands, and blacksmith Menelik is looking for financial security before proposing to his girlfriend

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: An intrepid team of four competes for cash by crossing a bridge of stepping stones across the studio floor. Quiz show, hosted by Ross Kemp

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers

programme.title: BBC News at Six; Weather
programme.desc: The latest headlines

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The highs and lows of life with the residents of Albert Square, Walford

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Antisemitism: Why British Jews Are Afraid - Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: The search for Northern Irelands Greatest Garden is almost at an end - five passionate gardeners have reached the final. After an early autumn road trip spent revisiting the five incredible gardens, Carol and Diarmuid join the finalists, their families and friends at Greenmounts walled garden in County Antrim. The pair reveal which one of the five will be named the winner

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Cat has to make a speech during the racecourses Ladies Day event, and it forces her to confront what shes been denying about her relationship with Dylan. With alcohol and adrenaline in the air, its not long before rivalries are stirred up and best behaviour gives way to chaos. Shannon and Arran are caught in the crossfire as their families violently collide

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Dylan rages at Shannon in the wake of Ladies Day, but she escapes and spends a life-changing day with Arran. Meanwhile, tensions erupt between Cat and Dylan, making them question the foundations of their life together. Can they find a way to salvage what theyve built, and will the family survive what comes next?

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: A round-up of todays headlines

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan joins officers from the Police Service of Northern Ireland as they patrol the streets of Belfast. Response officers Anna and Perri race to the aid of a man who has had bleach squirted directly on his face and into his eyes. Across town, Tom and Nigel race to the scene of a serious crash that has a left a pedestrian badly injured

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Sue Perkins hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with comedian Finlay Christie and journalist Camilla Long joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Brothers Iain and Kirk Russell from Glasgow present Suzie Fletcher with a leather football made famous by their grandfather, Rangers player Ian McMillan. It was used in the Scottish Cup final replay between Rangers and fierce local rivals Celtic back in 1963, a game that Rangers won 3-0. Bookbinder Chris Shaw is asked to restore a magazine written by the owners father when he was being held in a Japanese PoW camp, Geoffrey Harvey restores a pachinko machine - a pinball-style device - for a pair of sisters, and Rebecca Houghton works on a lyra - a bowled folk instrument - from Crete

programme.title: The Woman in Black (2012)
programme.desc: A lawyer tormented by the death of his wife is sent to a remote village to put deceased client Alice Drablows affairs in order. The locals are suspicious of outsiders and determined to keep the areas tragic history a secret, and the late Mrs Drablows isolated house appears to be haunted by a vengeful spirit that seems hell-bent on redressing a decades-past grievance. Horror based on Susan Hills novel, with Daniel Radcliffe and Ciaran Hinds

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand this apparent high-street epidemic, Stacey heads to Manchester to meet the people behind the crime and spends time on the shop floor with the staff trying to prevent further thefts

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna regrets persuading Gaz to try tantric sex when he gets hooked on it, while Louise wants a new family and seems to know exactly where to find one. Jonny gets a job as a farmer and Janet increases her home security

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Countless alien forces are drawn to a quiet backwater planet by a mysterious signal beaming out through time and space. The Doctor rescues Clara from a family Christmas dinner to help him discover what this message means, only to find that this insignificant planet has a terrible future, and that it falls to him to defend its peaceful inhabitants from the universes deadliest creatures. Matt Smith makes his final appearance as the Doctor in the 2013 Christmas special

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Socially awkward joke-shop owner Miranda is asked out to dinner by an old friend, prompting a visit to a clothing store to buy an outfit suitable for the date - but the expedition ends in disaster. First episode of the comedy, starring Miranda Hart, Sarah Hadland, Patricia Hodge and Sally Phillips. First aired in 2009

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: In a bid to encourage Gary to see her in a different light, Miranda joins an evening class to learn French, but is horrified to discover it is run by her former school teacher. The hapless singleton then decides a tango class is a more romantic option, only for her plans to take another turn for the worse. Comedy, guest starring Peter Davison, with Miranda Hart and Sarah Hadland

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Nugget stands up for himself. Priya tries to help Ravi. Lauren explores a new business venture

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together. Mark tries to help Lauren. Elaine and Ian attend a gala

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Suki starts to worry about the family. Max and Cindy grow closer. The Mitchells rally together

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max and Cindy share a moment. Ravi finally gets the help he needs. Lauren and Mark team up. Sam gives Mark an idea

programme.title: Four Weddings and a Funeral (1994)
programme.desc: A London mans misfortunes in love look set to change when he meets an American beauty. However, the prospect of a transatlantic relationship succeeding seems slim, so the pair go their separate ways - only for him to realise he is unable to get her out of his mind. Richard Curtiss comedy, with Hugh Grant, Andie MacDowell, John Hannah, Charlotte Coleman, Kristin Scott Thomas, Simon Callow and James Fleet

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Amber Haque explores the relationship between the Friends actor and Los Angeles-based drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen Jasveen Sangha - dubbed the Ketamine Queen by US prosecutors - who was one of five people charged in connection with Perrys untimely death, including two doctors and Perrys personal assistant. She pleaded guilty to five federal counts, including one of distributing ketamine resulting in death

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: The case of a Somerset high-flyer who turned his charm and connections into tools of deception, conning his friends and hundreds of others, while living a life of glamour himself. Through the voices of his victims and event industry insiders, this documentary uncovers the high personal and financial cost of chasing exclusivity, and how easily even the most cautious can be deceived

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Altered States
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores the changing face of US culture, beginning with a look at a movement in Portland, Oregon, that seeks to broaden how the world views marriage in a time of rising divorce rates, suggesting that monogamy is not essential and that polyamorous or open relationships can be equally fulfilling. However, while Louis meets many who have found happiness with multiple partners, he also discovers tales of heartbreak and jealousy

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Oloni has her work cut out with Vicky as she helps her find her perfect match, hoping to change her behaviour and her unrealistic criteria for her suitors

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: The former footballer heads deep into the Colombian jungle to find out why gold is now twice as valuable than cocaine to the countrys organised crime groups, meeting the young men drawn into this illegal venture, discovering the shocking health impact mining is having on them, as well as meeting the cartel gangsters who control almost every element of life in these remote regions

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to Sweden where he joins police at the front line of the ongoing gang war and meets with gang bosses in underground car parks. As trouble expands throughout the country, children as young as 13 are taking weapons and justice into their own hands, a photojournalist shows him their documentation of the murder scenes before he meets with a top gang leader for a training session involving pistol target practice in the dead of night

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to the rainforest in Ecuador in search of some of the deadliest creatures

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits Gabon, where a real-life King Kong shows him who is boss, and a scaly thief is caught red-handed

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey encourages members of the Patel family to tidy their immediate environment, as mum Bijal, dad Darshan and their two children move all their possessions into a warehouse, where they are encouraged to recycle, donate and sell items. There are emotional moments as the quartet go through their most treasured items. Bijal and Darshan struggle to part with their possessions, but really want to create an environment free of the chaos for their son, who finds it hard to concentrate in the clutter. As usual, home improvement gurus Dilly Carter, Iwan Carrington and Robert Bent offer tidying and upcycling tips

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Altered States
programme.desc: The documentary-maker explores the changing face of US culture, beginning with a look at a movement in Portland, Oregon, that seeks to broaden how the world views marriage in a time of rising divorce rates, suggesting that monogamy is not essential and that polyamorous or open relationships can be equally fulfilling. However, while Louis meets many who have found happiness with multiple partners, he also discovers tales of heartbreak and jealousy

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Dark States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Dark States - Murder in Milwaukee
programme.desc: The reporter examines gun crime and racial tensions in the US city, where the homicide rate is 12 times the national average and the police are frequently regarded with distrust. He follows the police departments homicide officers as they investigate a shooting, spends time with a family that has lost a loved one to gun crime, and meets a former gang leader who has become a social activist dedicated to finding solutions to her citys problems. He also examines the impact of a recent police shooting

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Part two of two. As Stacey continues to follow the emotional journey of the young women who have reported rape and now trying to navigate the justice system, she learns to appreciate that the statistics are stark. Fewer than two per cent of reported sexual assaults result in conviction, though this increases to over 50 per cent for cases that proceed to trial. As their trials near, the women learn that credibility also plays a pivotal role

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Dark States
programme.sub-title: Louis Theroux: Dark States - Murder in Milwaukee
programme.desc: The reporter examines gun crime and racial tensions in the US city, where the homicide rate is 12 times the national average and the police are frequently regarded with distrust. He follows the police departments homicide officers as they investigate a shooting, spends time with a family that has lost a loved one to gun crime, and meets a former gang leader who has become a social activist dedicated to finding solutions to her citys problems. He also examines the impact of a recent police shooting

programme.desc: Short film about four very different schoolgirls thrown together as they plan their escape from detention. Written by and starring Sabrina Ali

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sketch show set in Belfast, where the cats get stoned, the pigeons get philosophical and Gambo forgets how to breathe

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to the Philippines to find out if joining a gang is the only way to survive in the slum districts of the capital Manila. From street kids with few choices to families making homes among the graves of the citys notorious North Cemetery, Ashley discovers how gang life has changed in the Philippines and what factors still drive young men to join them

programme.desc: Extended highlights of a second-round match from day eight at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: The only daughter of a notorious criminal family is obsessed with the story of how her parents fell in love and hopes to find a great romance of her own. She finally thinks her luck has changed, but when she discovers who the man of her dreams is, she cannot be sure how her parents will react. Romantic crime drama, starring Emma Laird and Loyle Carner

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of Paul McCartneys original Hofner bass more than 50 years ago is one of rock n rolls great mysteries and this documentary tells the story of the instrument and the quest to find it. Featuring interviews with the former Beatle, his brother Mike, musicians Klaus Voormann and Elvis Costello, and roadies, journalists and fans who embarked on a mission to return the bass to its place in music history

programme.title: Andi Olivers Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi helps Jasper Thompson throws a party with a Caribbean flavour, to launch a community cafe at a pioneering Bristol homeless project that turns shipping containers into temporary housing. The chef must put together a feast that draws on her and Jaspers own roots in Antigua and Jamaica, whilst paying tribute to Bristols wider Caribbean heritage, using local produce

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents further live coverage of the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, as many of the approximately 59,000 amateur runners cross the finish line. programme.title: Live UEFA Womens Champions League
programme.desc: Arsenal v Lyon (Kick-off 3.30pm). All the action from the first leg of the semi-final, held at Emirates Stadium. Arsenal edged past London rivals Chelsea 3-2 on aggregate in the quarter-finals to maintain their chances of retaining the trophy. Lyon are record eight-time champions of Europe and scored three goals in extra time in the last eight to overcome VfL Wolfsburg 4-0 on the night and 4-1 over the two legs

programme.title: London Marathon
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the 46th staging of the 26.2-mile race, featuring the key moments from the elite races and a look at the stories of some of the record number of amateur runners. With commentary by Andrew Cotter, Steve Cram, Paula Radcliffe and Tanni Grey-Thompson, and reports from JJ Chalmers, Abby Cook, Tim Warwood and Harry Aikines-Aryeetey

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The Williamsons go for glory at their local show on Lewis, where their best sheep compete with the cream of the islands crop. Lucy and Adams baby arrives just as the work load ramps up, while Callum and Zara head south for a farm safety awareness event to commemorate the stories of farmers and their families affected by accidents at work

programme.title: Colombia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the country as 50 years of civil war draws to a close in the wake of a recently signed peace deal. He meets guerrilla fighters who have pledged to lay down arms, but also discovers a negative consequence of their disarming, as the paramilitary gangs that control the drugs trade grow in power. Simon travels through the country with coca farmers, who discuss the government support they will need in the turbulent times ahead, as poverty and land ownership becoming pressing issues

programme.title: Hell for Leather: The Story of Gaelic Football
programme.desc: The games profound connection to people and place, focusing on the fiercely contested Ulster final, which both divided and unified, and how Meath drew inspiration from its mythic past to finally overcome Dublin, aided by Seán Boylans legendary potions and lotions. The programme also recalls the time Mick ODwyer committed near heresy by coaching Kildare to beat his home country of Kerry and reach the 1988 All-Ireland final

programme.title: Inside Britains National Parks
programme.desc: The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys criss-crossed by clear rivers, with its western border adjoining Kielder, one of the largest man-made forests in Europe. This vast natural expanse is a haven for numerous bird species, including the osprey, once extinct in the UK, but now returning annually from Africa to breed, while the park is also rich in history, including extensive stretches of Hadrians Wall

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers talks to Sinn Fein president Mary Lou McDonald after her partys Ard Fheis in Belfast. Professor Jon Tonge from the University of Liverpool assesses Sinn Feins electoral chances

programme.title: The Chronicles of Armagh
programme.desc: In Tandragees Forthill Farm, Kenny Gracey reveals how his love of rare breeds has led to a second career, featuring his animals on the large and small screen. Wildlife ranger Donald is on foot patrol in Gosford forest park, searching for squirrel damage on the trees

programme.desc: Suzie Lee Arbuthnot creates a peach pudding that involves very little cooking

Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. With contributions from Helen Mirren, David Attenborough, Barack Obama, Tony Blair, and HM Queen Camilla

Theres a back to basics guide from Colin Crosbies plot just outside Dumfries, and a review of the gravel and grass garden, which will be thinned and replanted over the course of this season

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: Joanna Page is in the West Yorkshire town of Hebden Bridge, where friends Emma and Sophie compete to raise money for Dove House Hospice, picking up items including a vintage St Michael jacket, a rare red rotary dial telephone, a 1970s Holly Hobbie doll and a colourful 1960s picnic set

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals the hidden wild world of a country garden in Cumbria. A barn swallow returns to its birthplace to prepare a new nest, field mice sneak under the noses of predators, a newt lays eggs in the pond, and a mole barrels through his tunnel network in search of a female. But Cumbria is the wettest county in England, and when the weather breaks, things take a turn for the worse for the animals

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leeds United. Action from the second semi-final at Wembley Stadium . Leeds only triumph in this competition was back in 1972 and they defeated West Ham United on penalties in the previous round

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Tokyo on a sleek Shinkansen bullet train and explores the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. On board a sleek Shinkansen bullet train, Michael arrives in Tokyo, one of the greatest cities on earth, with a population of 37 million in the metropolitan area. He begins by exploring the ornate Akasaka Palace, built in 1909 by Emperor Meiji for his son, the crown prince Yoshihito. Hes surprised to find that, with its neo-baroque architecture and impressive fountains, it looks more than a little British. In the fashionable district of Ginza, Michael journeys underground to ride the metro, where he is intrigued by the catchy jingles played at each station and meets one of the composers behind 300 of the tunes

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal presents live coverage of the evening session on day 10 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the second round concludes with the final frames of two matches. For the fifth time, all three members of the Class of 92 - Ronnie OSullivan, John Higgins, and Mark Williams - progressed to the quarter-finals in 2025, overcoming Pang Junxu, Xiao Guodong and Hossein Vafaei respectively. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Ken Doherty, Steven Hallworth, Stephen Hendry, John Parrott, Joe Perry, Dennis Taylor and Lee Walker

In Irish

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.sub-title: Pregnant and Jailed in Post Office Scandal
programme.desc: On her first day running her local Post Office, Seema discovered a cash shortfall at the till. She had to put in her own money to make the books add up. Every day, more and more money appeared to be missing. She didnt know it then, but she was the latest victim of the infamous Post Office Horizon accounting scandal - and eventually she was convicted and sent to prison, while pregnant. Here, Bill pairs her with artist Jack, whose detailed paintings explore resilience, as she tells her remarkable story

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: At 1am in Cumbria, a 999 call reports a gang breaking into a supermarket cash machine using high-powered cutting tools. They work quickly, escaping with thousands before police arrive. Its the second ATM targeted in the region within 24 hours. Seventy miles away, news of the raids is shared with West Midlands Police, who are already pursuing a prolific gang linked to similar attacks nationwide. As the task force builds intelligence, Cumbria Police lead the forensic investigation, until a third raid heightens the urgency of the case

programme.desc: The units are packed full in Stanton, as Darrell has to deal with Kimbers eyes on a competitor. Ivy brings a full truck, hoping for a second haul. Dusty unpacks what could be the ultimate assistant, while Renes hunch serves him well

programme.desc: Andy and Paul return to Mount Shasta, California, to investigate the cause of the strange and potentially deadly energies that seem to draw everything toward the mountain - from people to Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena

programme.desc: After the team unearths new and bizarre clues from the Mesa on Skinwalker Ranch, a complex laser experiment leads to a shocking encounter

programme.title: A Ship Through Time
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sean Fletcher investigates the secrets of a shipwrecked 15th-century trading galleon found by accident off Newport in 2002, her international significance and how two of Europes most turbulent and violent wars shaped her fate. He discovers what this trading galleon carried, and to where and a possible powerful owner. He also sheds light on a possible connection to the Wars of the Roses

programme.title: The Witches of Salem
programme.desc: The hysteria that unfolded during the Salem witch trials, leading to the execution of 20 women in an affluent New England community. Mark Strong narrates a real-life horror story, with young teenage girls tragically accused and convicted by the shadowy adults lurking in the background. The series draws on extensive historical documents and court records to bring this drama to life

programme.title: Historys Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: An ancient mechanical device found on a 2,000-year-old shipwreck has become known as the worlds first computer. The machine is centuries more advanced than historians thought possible, but its origin and true purpose are shrouded in secrecy

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Unforgettable moments featuring WWE superstars, highlighting the tag teams of the 1990s

programme.title: WWEs Greatest Moments

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: UFO Over the Andes, the Ohio Grassman and Ancient Horn Mystery
programme.desc: The legendary Ohio Grassman is apparently captured in footage of a tall, dark-coloured, hairy creature walking through woods. Plus, a look at the Chilean family photograph of a real UFO flying above the Andes Mountains and the notion that a 5,000-year-old horn discovered on a beach in England belongs to a prehistoric creature that still lives

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Men in Black Calls, Old Kentucky Aliens and Mysterious Eclipse
programme.desc: Tony Harris and his team of experts examine several seemingly impossible events, such as a family managing to survive a bolt of lightning ripping through their car during a Kansas storm, a bizarre creature spotted on a driveway camera and an image captured of an alien probe

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Teen UFO, Flying Dinosaur Over Idaho, and Headless Goats
programme.desc: A photograph taken by a Swedish teenager said to offer some of the most convincing UFO evidence ever. Plus, a video posted online showing a pterodactyl-like creature flying over Idaho and the reason headless goats are repeatedly being dumped in an Atlanta River

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts tells the story of Viking England by studying the history of York. She takes part in a Viking battle, meets the metal detectorists behind one of the UKs greatest archaeological finds and gets her hands on an almost 1,000-year-old poo. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson takes to the skies to see how the current layout of York reflects its history

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the Norman period of British history by examining events that occurred in Winchester, a historic town that still retains many hallmarks of the 11th and 12th centuries. To get a taste of this era, she attempts to storm a castle wearing chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery, and tries some eel pie. At the conclusion of the episode, cutting edge CGI is used to take the town back in time, and recreate how it would have looked during this period of British history

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Documentary following pioneering surgeons at Royal Marsden Hospital as they face some of the most complex and challenging oncological cases in the world. Other surgeons perform a complex procedure to save a photographers arm, and remove new cancer growth in a patients abdomen

programme.desc: A desperate Ray does everything he can to get to the front of the queue to meet the New York Mets 1969 team at the National Baseball Hall of Fame. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.sub-title: The Ann Who Came to Dinner

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The Cranes take a trip to the mountains for a holiday away from the city, but Frasier, Niles and Daphne are plagued by nightmares during the overnight journey. Martin also has a strange dream, but unlike the rest of the family, continues to sleep soundly

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles buys an expensive sweater, despite promising Daphne he would start saving for their childs college fund, and Frasier agrees to get Roz and Alice tickets to see a show by his ex-wife, which only serves to fan the embers of lust. Laurie Metcalf guest stars

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Martin suffers a mild heart attack, but tries to keep it from Ronee - to the dismay of Frasier and Niles, who feel it would be unfair of him to hide such serious news. Eventually worn down by their persistence, he decides to confide in her - leading to a conversation that ends in an unexpected way

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Midday news summary

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon OBrien and Henry Cole meet Gaby, whose garden is strewn with interesting items. From a mass of tangled weeds, Henry pulls out a vintage sack barrow, while Simon finds a shepherds stove hidden in the undergrowth

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Matt, Janice and Mike head to historic Cragside house and gardens in Northumberland, the original smart home powered by ingenious 19th-century hydro-electric inventions. Janice and Matt get to grips with a Victorian dishwasher, while at Cragend Farm, the Bakers discover an agricultural machine which inspired the design of Londons Tower Bridge

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: BandB owners Andy and Julian enlist the help of Laura Hamilton in finding their dream home away from home in Gran Canaria on a budget of £125,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: The owners of a rural retreat in Andalucia prepare to host their first wedding, while in France, expansion is on the cards for Colin and Christina who left behind office jobs to set up a gîte and glamping business. In Italy, Jane and Walter face a difficult decision about their future at their unique BandB

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In Brittany, Donna and Paul race to finish their utility room, while in Normandy at Château de Lalacelle, Terry and Ashley turn their attention to the front gardens, with ambitious water features planned and kilometres of pipework to lay. Meanwhile in the Dordogne, moving day brings a startling discovery for Helen and Malcolm: a cellar packed with 20,000 bottles

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners help Trevor and Asia find a place big enough for all their travel treasures, and the Hamiltons find Gene and Theresa a place with a bedroom that passes the shimmy test

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Dog behaviour specialist Sue pulls out all the stops for Woodgreens most rejected dogs. Barley the labradoodle hopes his latest liaison might prove to be third time lucky. Elsewhere, its a rollercoaster of emotions as beloved long-term resident Lady, a Staffie cross, finally has a chance of finding a home after a year of searching

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: Documentary following 12 adult virgins as they embark on a unique course in intimacy at a luxury retreat, to see if it can help them overcome the fears. The group meet for the first time and begin to unpack their deepest fears and anxieties. But with shame running deep and barriers high, can any of them open up and take the first step toward intimacy?

programme.title: The Worlds Tallest Man: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Documentary about a man who was declared the tallest in the world in 2010, but continues to grow due to a rare condition that could kill him. Fifteen years later, this film explores his circumstances after life-saving surgery. Doctors examine the toll that his towering body has had on his health, while Guinness World Records measures him again to see if he still holds the title that made him famous

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Following three patients who have adapted to the challenges thrown their way, including 27-year-old Josh, who was rushed to AandE after being thrown from his motorbike at speed. Georgie has brought in her 10-month-old son Phoenix after she caught him sucking on a bleach tablet, while 21-year-old Ross is in AandE after dislocating his shoulder at work

programme.title: Rescue: Extreme Medics
programme.desc: The air ambulance is dispatched to two serious road traffic collisions, while on the west bank of Loch Lomond, a minibus carrying 15 tourists has collided with a car. In South Ayrshire, an 86-year-old grandmother is struggling to breathe after a high-speed crash, and 72-year-old William has a severe cut to his arm after an attempt to chop wood with a circular saw went horrifically wrong

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Grayson concludes his tour of Silicon Valley by considering the motivations of the men behind the technology, such as Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos - and how their values might be challenged. He meets a scientist working on empathetic robots which offer a more human-centric alternative to the priorities of the industry and encounters one of the key players in AI, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, grappling with the possibility that AI could be shaped in such a way to offer a more optimistic future

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: In London, a 90-year-old sings the Frank Sinatra classic that gave him back his voice after he had a stroke. A 15-year-old performs an original tune about growing up Black in Britain. An 83-year-old from Derry sings a song that she gave up 30 years ago because it made her daughter cry, and a father and son perform the lullaby thats always been their song

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of dinner-party hosts from Maidstone, Kent, compete, beginning with drama school owners Lisa and Iain, who cook an entirely Scottish menu. On the second night, Hannah and Paul fail to wow their guests with a Middle Eastern and Moroccan menu, while the final soiree sees Ling and Mark prepare food they like to call Chinglish, which combines Lings Chinese heritage and Marks English roots

programme.title: Gordon Ramsays Secret Service
programme.desc: Gordons late-night investigation of Mrs Whites Golden Rule Cafe turns up shocking results, and a bombshell reveals a secret financial crisis. Will the 101-year-old proprietor and her 75-year-old son Larry ever hand over the reins and retire?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef comes to the aid of a French restaurant in the heart of Moorpark, California. The pompous, over-complicated cuisine is keeping the customers away and there is an imminent threat of bankruptcy. However, the owner is vigorously opposed to change, despite the failings of his current business model

programme.title: Guy Martins Best Bits
programme.sub-title: Guy Martins World Speed Records (edited version)
programme.desc: The presenter looks back at times when he helped to build and pilot amazing machines to world speed records, and tells the fascinating stories behind the record attempts

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of dinner-party hosts from Plymouth compete, beginning with newlyweds Nicole and Nathan, who offer a fine-dining experience. programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver showcases a one-pan recipe to provide a perfect solution for busy life - a foolproof traybaked salmon in a bag

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home

Later, he travels to the Peak District, where he comes across a farmhouse which is in desperate need of a transformation

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer sells his soul to the Devil in return for a doughnut, a gremlin attacks Ottos bus while Bart is on his way to school, and the family sets out to end a vampiric Mr Burns reign of terror

programme.title: The King of Queens

Janice and Matt get to grips with a Victorian dishwasher, while at Cragend Farm, the Bakers discover an agricultural machine which inspired the design of Londons Tower Bridge

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with the Rev Kate Bottley in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against words and numbers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: BandB owners Andy and Julian enlist the help of Laura Hamilton in finding their dream home away from home in Gran Canaria on a budget of £125,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: The owners of a rural retreat in Andalucia prepare to host their first wedding, while in France, expansion is on the cards for Colin and Christina who left behind office jobs to set up a gîte and glamping business. In Italy, Jane and Walter face a difficult decision about their future at their unique BandB

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: In Brittany, Donna and Paul race to finish their utility room, while in Normandy at Château de Lalacelle, Terry and Ashley turn their attention to the front gardens, with ambitious water features planned and kilometres of pipework to lay. Meanwhile in the Dordogne, moving day brings a startling discovery for Helen and Malcolm: a cellar packed with 20,000 bottles

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: The Garners help Trevor and Asia find a place big enough for all their travel treasures, and the Hamiltons find Gene and Theresa a place with a bedroom that passes the shimmy test

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Dog behaviour specialist Sue pulls out all the stops for Woodgreens most rejected dogs. Barley the labradoodle hopes his latest liaison might prove to be third time lucky. Elsewhere, its a rollercoaster of emotions as beloved long-term resident Lady, a Staffie cross, finally has a chance of finding a home after a year of searching

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: Documentary following 12 adult virgins as they embark on a unique course in intimacy at a luxury retreat, to see if it can help them overcome the fears. The group meet for the first time and begin to unpack their deepest fears and anxieties. But with shame running deep and barriers high, can any of them open up and take the first step toward intimacy?

programme.title: The Worlds Tallest Man: The Next Chapter
programme.desc: Documentary about a man who was declared the tallest in the world in 2010, but continues to grow due to a rare condition that could kill him. Fifteen years later, this film explores his circumstances after life-saving surgery. Doctors examine the toll that his towering body has had on his health, while Guinness World Records measures him again to see if he still holds the title that made him famous

programme.title: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programme.desc: Documentary following pioneering surgeons at Royal Marsden Hospital as they face some of the most complex and challenging oncological cases in the world. A surgical robot is used to treat a tumour on a patients voice box, in a procedure that has never been attempted before. Other surgeons perform a complex procedure to save a photographers arm, and remove new cancer growth in a patients abdomen

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The Essex crew and Herts Air Ambulance race to help a teenager, who has a major brain injury after colliding with a car in Hemel Hempstead. In Bicester, Dimis heart is racing dangerously fast and needs to be shocked back into a normal rhythm and in Clacton, some DIY goes wrong for Martin, who has a suspected broken collarbone

programme.title: Wonders of the World I Cant See
programme.desc: Blind comedian Chris McCausland explores some of the worlds most iconic landmarks to discover things that the ordinary traveller might miss. Chris is joined by Harry Hill for a trip to the Acropolis in the Greek capital Athens. They soak up some local culture, including sea fishing, pottery making, olive oil tasting and Greek drama lessons, and even try their hand at a few Olympic sports in the iconic Panathenaic Stadium

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of the Olde Stone Mill restaurant in Tuckahoe, New York, which is struggling to attract customers, leading to debts of nearly $1million. Gordon feels the owner is more interested in his own appearance than how his food tastes and a screaming match ensues

programme.title: Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future
programme.desc: Grayson concludes his tour of Silicon Valley by considering the motivations of the men behind the technology, such as Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Jeff Bezos - and how their values might be challenged. He meets a scientist working on empathetic robots which offer a more human-centric alternative to the priorities of the industry and encounters one of the key players in AI, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark, grappling with the possibility that AI could be shaped in such a way to offer a more optimistic future

programme.title: Your Song
programme.desc: In London, a 90-year-old sings the Frank Sinatra classic that gave him back his voice after he had a stroke. A 15-year-old performs an original tune about growing up Black in Britain. An 83-year-old from Derry sings a song that she gave up 30 years ago because it made her daughter cry, and a father and son perform the lullaby thats always been their song

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three sets of dinner-party hosts from Maidstone, Kent, compete, beginning with drama school owners Lisa and Iain, who cook an entirely Scottish menu. On the second night, Hannah and Paul fail to wow their guests with a Middle Eastern and Moroccan menu, while the final soiree sees Ling and Mark prepare food they like to call Chinglish, which combines Lings Chinese heritage and Marks English roots

programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie cooks one-pan recipes that offer the perfect solutions for busy lives. Here he makes a dukkah roast lamb feast with a tip to speed up cooking

programme.title: Hollyoaks Omnibus
programme.desc: A shocking discovery for Ste forces a confrontation, Jack finds himself in the middle of his feuding relatives, and a teenager refuses to take orders from their mum. Meanwhile, at The Lofts club night, Mercedes is loved-up and Cleos ready to let off steam, and Rex goes public wearing womens clothes. Mercedes advises Theresa on how to handle Kathleen-Angel, Ro tries to comfort Rex, and tension sizzles between two former flames

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Teams captains Jon Richardson and Sean Lock are joined by guests Louie Spence, Joe Wilkinson, Elis James and Shappi Khorsandi, who answer questions based on opinion polls carried out around the country to see who best understands what is going on in Britain today. Irreverent panel show hosted by Jimmy Carr

programme.desc: Australian version of the prank show, starring comedy duo Matt Ford and Jack Steele. They act as receptionists and create chaos among the clients, and also confuse customers while posing as enlightened wellness gurus in a yoga studio

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra shocks Ray by revealing she would like more time alone, making him fear their marriage may be heading for the rocks. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra plays amateur psychiatrist to discover why a convalescing Robert is driving everyone crazy with his mean-spirited behaviour. American comedy, starring Brad Garrett and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra suffers from PMT, so Ray decides to help alleviate her symptoms - but only succeeds in making matters worse. Ray Romano stars

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert returns to work after sick leave, but is soon driven to the conclusion that he does not have the nerve to be a policeman any more and decides to pursue a safer career in telemarketing

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Robert bumps into his ex-wife, and considers rekindling the relationship. Meanwhile, Frank tells Amy the story of how the pair split up in the first place

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Part one of two. Marie surprises everyone by arranging a trip to Rome for the whole family, but all Ray can do is complain. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Doris Roberts

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One in Vegas - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Rachel makes Ross regret playing a prank on her, while Monica and Chandler have a spot of good luck and Joey tries out his latest get-rich-quick scheme. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston, David Schwimmer and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross and Rachel wake up the morning after their impromptu marriage and regret the hasty circumstances of the ceremony, while Monica and Chandler find that neither of them really wants to rush to the altar. Joey and Phoebe have a cross-country adventure. Comedy, starring David Schwimmer, Jennifer Aniston, Lisa Kudrow and Matt LeBlanc

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Hugs Rachel
programme.desc: Monica and Chandler decide to move in together, but have difficulty explaining to Rachel that she will not be a part of the arrangement. Meanwhile, Phoebe begs Ross to get his latest marriage annulled. American comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross encourages Chandler and Monica to move in together and makes a dramatic decision about Rachel. David Schwimmer, Matthew Perry, Courteney Cox and Jennifer Aniston star

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Joey Loses His Insurance
programme.desc: Ross tries his hand at teaching and adopts an English accent in the process. Joey suffers a hernia and Rachel discovers her marriage has not been annulled. American comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc, David Schwimmer and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey is mistaken for the owner of a Porsche and uses the mix-up to enhance his image. Rachel forces Ross to annul their marriage, and Phoebe tries her best to look after the triplets. American comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One on the Last Night
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler celebrate their last night as flatmates, and Ross claims he is too busy babysitting to help Monica as she frantically tries to arrange Rachels packing before the removal firm arrives. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An Australian dancer (guest star Elle Macpherson) moves in with Joey and he struggles to refrain from flirting. Chandler tries to impress Monica by cleaning their flat, and Rachel is embarrassed by Phoebes jogging style. Comedy, with Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe claims she kissed Ralph Lauren at Rachels workplace, and room-mate Janine encourages Joey to get in touch with his feminine side, much to Chandlers annoyance. Ross bleaches his teeth in an effort to impress a date. Comedy, guest starring Elle Macpherson

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Got High
programme.desc: Monicas parents arrive for Thanksgiving, leading to trouble for Chandler, who discovers they dont like him because they think he uses drugs. Fortunately, their disapproval soon focuses on Ross instead. Comedy, guest starring Elliott Gould and Christina Pickles

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey tries to find the perfect opportunity to kiss Janine, while Ross and Monica resurrect their high-school dance routine for a TV programme. Comedy, guest starring Elle Macpherson

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with the Apothecary Table
programme.desc: Joey finally dates Janine, but she does not like his friends. Rachel worries that Phoebe will not appreciate the way she has furnished their apartment owing to her environmental conscience. Comedy, guest starring Elle Macpherson

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebes frank attitude causes Monica and Rachel to lose their tempers. Joey reluctantly accepts a job as a waiter at Central Perk, while Ross and Chandler argue over which of them made up a raunchy joke

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gives advice on being more independent to her pampered younger sister (Reese Witherspoon), who promptly makes her regret it by flirting with Ross. Meanwhile, Joey gets in trouble for giving free food to female customers at Central Perk

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Cant Cry
programme.desc: Phoebe accidentally becomes a porn icon, Rachel tries to stop her sister from embarking on a relationship with Ross, and Chandler reveals he is unable to cry. Guest starring Reese Witherspoon

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One That Could Have Been - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. A revelation from Rachel prompts the pals to ponder what might have been if their lives had taken different courses. Comedy, starring Jennifer Aniston and Courteney Cox

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: The comedian hosts the Saturday-night game show, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing a cash prize. programme.title: Impractical Jokers
programme.desc: The guys pretend to be overprotective parents, and disclose perplexing side effects, with one Joker being unable hack it and learning that not all that glitters is gold

programme.title: Alan Carr Live: Spexy Beast
programme.desc: A performance from the comedians 2011 tour, recorded in front of an audience of 13,000 fans at Manchester Arena. He tells tales of how becoming a TV personality has changed his life, discusses the perils of moving from Manchester to London, and recalls encounters with unusual guests on his talk show Chatty Man. He also shares stories of his less high-profile dramas, including the challenges involved in owning an eccentric dog

programme.title: Iain Stirlings Roast the Internet

programme.desc: As the wedding planning show gets an upgrade, grooms-to-be are now given £14,000 to plan every detail of their nuptials - although they must still do so without any input from their significant others. Jennis confidence has taken a knock since she developed a disability caused by pregnancy, and while Andrew became her dutiful carer, he admits romance has taken a back-seat, and hopes an extravagant wedding will help him show his fiancee the spark is still there

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Steve takes turning 40 as his cue to seize the initiative in arranging his wedding to partner Nicki. However, unbeknownst to her would-be-husband, Nicki only pretended to be a fellow rocker to catch Steves eye. programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride
