programme.desc: A Nazi war criminal masquerades as a college professor in an American town, while a government official is intent on tracking him down. Drama, directed by and starring Orson Welles, with Loretta Young and Edward G Robinson

programme.title: Roy Orbison Forever
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the singer-songwriter, charting his career and relationships with other musicians through interviews and archive performances. Featuring interviews with Elvis Costello, Tom Petty, Bono, Gerry Marsden, Jeff Lynne, Olivia Harrison and Barbara Orbison

programme.title: Opry Live
programme.desc: A selection of performances from world-famous Nashville music venue the Grand Ole Opry, featuring Keith Urban, Scotty McCreery and 49 Winchester

programme.title: Jamie Cullum Live at Blenheim Palace
programme.desc: A 2004 concert at the Oxfordshire stately home by the jazz musician and his band, featuring songs by Cole Porter, Jimi Hendrix, Alan Jay Lerner, Radiohead and N.E.R.D

programme.title: Seal Plays Baloise Session
programme.desc: The singer performs at the Swiss indoor festival in 2024, featuring a selection of jazz standards as well as hits including Killer, Kiss from a Rose and Crazy

programme.title: Eddy Grant: Live in London
programme.desc: A 1986 concert by the musician at the South Banks Festival Gardens

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music journalists examine the life and career of the musician, whose hits include You Are the Sunshine of My Life and Superstition

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Music journalists examine the life and music of the American singer-songwriter, whose hits included Oh, Pretty Woman, Only the Lonely and Crying

programme.title: A Midsummer Nights Dream
programme.desc: John Neumeiers production of the ballet based on Shakespeares play, weaving a tangled web of love, jealousy and magic as fairies, mortals and rustics collide in a dreamlike world

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials for budding artists, with each edition focusing on a different subject or technique, while Tai-Shan Schierenberg has tips on painting portraits

programme.title: Artist of the Year: Masterclass
programme.desc: Tutorials by painters from both Portrait and Landscape Artist of the Year series, helping viewers understand the basics of painting and drawing

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man with a remarkable memory plans to turn his talent into cash by offering to help someone recall a locker number. Starring Bernard Cribbins, Colin Blakely and Judy Geeson

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A near-bankrupt hotel owner goes to great lengths to get her hands on an inheritance from a rich aunt. Starring Hayley Mills, Stuart Wilson and Phyllida Law

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A struggling deputy DA gets a murder conviction against a man for killing his wife, but is confronted by another who claims he committed the crime. With Pat Hingle and Georgann Johnson

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: Eddie Slovak meets an old Army buddy named Pete who once saved his life. He intends to repay his friend. He offers him money for a business, but Peter refuses. Eddie tries to talk to Petes wife Louise. Louise falls for Eddie and the two become romantically involved. With Alex Nicol and Nita Talbot

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, who first rose to fame for his work with Orson Welles on Citizen Kane, The Magnificent Ambersons and Journey into Fear

programme.title: Andre Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: The violinist reflects on the highlights of his concerts in Mexico, including the Platin Tenors performance of Granada

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A man has an unpleasant shock when he learns his recently deceased father owed a large amount of money. Starring Evelyn Laye, Richard Pearson, Hugh Fraser and David Webb

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A sheriff investigates a murder, but encounters problems when it turns out the main suspect has an identical twin. Starring David Cassidy and Darren McGavin

programme.title: Bagpipes: A Celebration
programme.desc: Documentary bringing together the sounds of bands, drums and solo pipers in a series of spectacular performances set against the breathtaking landscapes of Scotland. Weaving together powerful performances with the hustle and bustle of backstage, this programme offers a deep dive into the passion, history and traditions of Scotlands most iconic musical art form, with individual pipers discussing their connection to the tunes played, the locations, and the art of playing

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Insight into the making of Elvis Presleys 1956 self-titled debut album, featuring contributions by some of the original supporting musicians, as well as friends and colleagues including Keith Richards and BB King. The programme includes footage of Presley performing classic tracks such as Heartbreak Hotel and Blue Suede Shoes, and examines the records production

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the actor, who won Best Actor Oscars for his roles in My Left Foot, There Will Be Blood and Lincoln

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Joan Bakewell and Frank Skinner head to Cardiff, where 12 artists anxiously set up their easels at the Senedd. The contenders have just four hours to capture the likenesses of comedian and actor Greg Davies, Paralympian and broadcaster Ade Adepitan and former rugby union and judo international Non Evans, while last years winner Nick Lord reveals how victory in the competition changed his life

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An American airman returns to England for a nostalgic visit, but his plans turn sour. Starring Van Johnson, Geoffrey Bayldon, Margaraet Courtenay and Anne Carroll

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An elderly aristocrat recalls a duel fought in Germany in the 1920s, and reveals a shocking secret. Drama, starring Peter Cushing

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A husband gets locked in a secure office with the body of his beautiful young mistress, whom he has just murdered. Drama, with Joseph Cotten and Christine White

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: A man fears his mentally unstable daughter has bought a gun to kill herself, but he might have it all wrong. Drama, starring Ann Todd and John McIntire

programme.title: Andre Rieu: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: The violinist reflects on a colourful tribute to Mary Poppins as he rummages through the elaborate costumes in his studios wardrobe

programme.title: Andre Rieu: Love Is All Around
programme.desc: The violinist and his Johann Strauss Orchestra perform in his home town of Maastricht, playing a repertoire that spans classical, operatic and popular music

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: Barry Cryer pays tribute to comedy actor Sid James, who rose to fame in Hancocks Half Hour and became a mainstay of the Carry On franchise

programme.title: Yuja x Hockney at Lightroom London
programme.desc: A concert in which internationally acclaimed classical pianist Yuja Wang plays pieces inspired by her responsed to David Hockneys artworks

programme.title: Greatest Albums Live
programme.desc: Gregory Porter and his band perform the Grammy-winning Liquid Spirit - his biggest selling album yet - at Londons Middle Eight Hotel

programme.title: Discovering Music
programme.desc: Friends, fans and fellow musicians reflect on Elton Johns career, exploring his greatest hits, as well as his work on The Lion King and Billy Elliot

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton arrests the prime suspect in the shooting of his three fellow law-enforcers. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin and Andre Braugher

programme.title: True Detective
programme.desc: As Prior digs into the links between Tsalal and mining company Silver Sky, Navarro rescues Leah from a local protest that has turned violent. At a meeting with Silver Sky executve Kate McKitterick, Danvers balks when Connelly shares the official cause of the Tsalal mens deaths - and gets a warning against pursuing the case any further. Later, Hank receives a quid pro quo offer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Pembleton helps to cover up a scandal, only to find it coming back to haunt him. Gritty police drama, starring Andre Braugher, Daniel Baldwin and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Russert learns one of his former partners is being transferred back into Homicide. Pembleton chooses not to implicate the commissioner in the case that cost him his job. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Part one of three. Howard, Felton and Bolander are ambushed and shot as they try to arrest a suspect. Gritty police drama, starring Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Richard Belzer

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: After shocking news, Sabi takes some time to process the situation - but their absence soon causes loved ones to worry

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor heads to New Orleans and meets soul singer Irma Thomas at the Hotel de Monteleone, an establishment that was popular with writers such as Tennessee Williams, F Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway. Richard speaks to Steel Magnolias author Robert Harling about the Soniat House, check into the supposedly haunted Hotel Maison de Ville and talks to burlesque dancer Chrissy Owens at the Roosevelt

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Swearengen strikes a departure deal with Miss Isringhausen and orders Farnum to befriend Blasanov so he can monitor any telegrams coming in from Yankton. Bullock gains possession of a safe full of money, while Hostetler and the General acquire a valuable wild horse they hope to sell to the cavalry. Drama, starring Ian McShane and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The town is left reeling in the wake of Williams accident, and Hostetler and the General flee in fear of retribution once word gets out that the horse was in their care. Meanwhile, Swearengen finally receives word from Yankton. Drama, starring Ian McShane, Brad Dourif and Timothy Olyphant

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: The residents of Deadwood face a sad day when Williams funeral is held, and Seth Bullock and his wife prepare to leave the town following their sons tragic death. Meanwhile, Alma offers her answer to Ellsworth. Timothy Olyphant and Anna Gunn star

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: A nervous Alma and Ellsworth get married, and Tolliver informs George Hearst of Wolcotts murderous acts. When the engineer is confronted, he takes drastic action with devastating consequences. Wu launches an attack on Lee

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: Tell Your God to Ready for Blood
programme.desc: Seth Bullock and bartender Harry Manning compete for the office of sheriff, and EB Farnum and Sol Star campaign to become mayor. Meanwhile, Swearengen continues to view George Hearst with suspicion. Western drama set in a lawless US town, starring Ian McShane

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.sub-title: I Am Not the Fine Man You Take Me For
programme.desc: While competition for the roles of sheriff and mayor is hotting up, Jane achieves a captive audience the candidates can only dream of. Swearengen receives a cryptic note informing him of another bloody incident at the Gem, but Hurst later makes his intentions known in a more straightforward message. Meanwhile, Andy Cramed pays an unexpected visit to Tolliver. Starring Ian McShane and Robin Weigert

programme.title: Deadwood
programme.desc: Emmy award-winner Brian Cox joins the cast as a flamboyant stage promoter on a mission to introduce a modicum of culture to the residents of the gold-rush town. Hearsts long-serving cook also makes an appearance as the Grand Central chef, and Wu returns with news of a soon-to-arrive group of imported workers. Meanwhile, Bullock uncovers the truth about the Gem killings. Cleo King guest stars

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: In April 1986, workers and firefighters put their lives on the line to try to deal with an explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant near the city of Pripyat in Ukraine. Fact-based drama, starring Jared Harris, Emily Watson and Stellan Skarsgard

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: With millions of lives already at risk, Ulana races to warn leading Soviet nuclear physicist Valery Legasov about the threat of second, devastating explosion

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov draws up a plan, but it comes with great risks. Meanwhile, Lyudmilla ignores warnings about her firefighter husbands contamination

programme.title: Chernobyl
programme.desc: Valery Legasov and Boris Shcherbina come up with a life-saving idea to remove radioactive debris. Elsewhere, Ulana faces problems as she investigates the explosion

programme.desc: Valery Legasov, Boris Shcherbina and Ulana Khomyuk risk their lives and their reputations to expose the truth about the Chernobyl disaster

programme.desc: Howard returns to work, still feeling weak after the shooting, but solves a murder case from her desk to prove her mettle. Gritty police drama, starring Melissa Leo

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming heads across the Irish Sea to Dublin, where he succumbs to the citys famous charm and meets some of its most fascinating characters. On a journey that takes in an 18th-century pleasure house and a burlesque life-painting class, Alan also stops off in Temple Bar for a pint with singer Sharon Corr, unlocks a mystery in Irelands oldest library and uncovers a stylish secret that lurks behind the steel shutters of an old lock-up

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Glasgow, one of his favourite cities, to reveal unusual and often hidden sights and sounds overlooked by the public. Starting at the Necropolis, he travels to the worlds oldest music hall before enjoying the views from a city-centre landmark. He also finds the time to take tea with a film star

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Life in the Devon fishing port of Brixham. Spring is in the air, and the town is a hive of activity as skippers return their pleasure yachts to the water after winter in the dry dock, while the pressure is on for Brixham to look its best for the arrival of Princess Anne, who will officially open the new £20million fish market, and the start of the summer season

programme.title: Fish Town
programme.desc: Brixham hosts its annual Pirate Festival, paying tribute to Devons colourful maritime history, while fisherman Nathan sets off in search of a lucrative catch

programme.desc: Howard returns to work, still feeling weak after the shooting, but solves a murder case from her desk to prove her mettle. Gritty police drama, starring Melissa Leo

programme.title: Deadwood

programme.title: Deadwood

Meanwhile, two more actors arrive in town to be reunited with Langrishe as the troupe prepares to transform Chez Ami into a theatre. Ian McShane stars

programme.desc: Documentary following stately home owners who hire out their estates to earn extra income, and the clients who pay to use their properties for functions including stag parties, weddings and hunting trips. programme.desc: Drama telling the story of the fictional Pope Pius XIII, the Catholic Churchs first American pontiff, as he struggles to establish his papacy. Starring Jude Law

programme.title: Billions
programme.desc: Axe and Chuck fight for the loyalty of the same witness. Taylor travels to Silicon Valley to explore new business opportunities

programme.title: Euphoria
programme.desc: Drama offering a look at the complexities of life in a social-media obsessed world, starring Zendaya, Jacob Elordi and Sydney Sweeney

programme.title: Heated Rivalry
programme.desc: Rival ice hockey stars Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov face an unexpected emotional connection, challenging their professional paths and disrupting the sporting worlds expectations. Romantica drama, starring Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie

programme.title: The Last of Us

programme.title: The Last of Us
programme.desc: As the WLF makes a dangerous discovery, Ellie faces a key decision. An unlikely source shares a shocking truth

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: A hostage crisis leads to a 16-year-old murder case being reopened, while Pembleton and Bayliss investigate an elderly man whose wife drowned in the bath. Gritty police drama on the streets of Baltimore, starring Daniel Baldwin, Ned Beatty and Clark Johnson

programme.title: Homicide: Life on the Street
programme.desc: Howard inherits a crime she cannot solve when Crosettis caseload is distributed - especially as the latest piece of evidence has gone missing. Gritty police drama, starring Melissa Leo, Daniel Baldwin and Richard Belzer

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Documentary following stately home owners who hire out their estates to earn extra income, and the clients who pay to use their properties for functions including stag parties, weddings and hunting trips. The programme explores how the reality of life in Britains grand houses is often less glamorous than it appears, as proprietors struggle to maintain crumbling buildings and pay utility bills, and examines the modern-day divide between domestic staff and their employers. Featuring life at Chillingham Castle, Maunsel House, Carlton Towers and Muncaster Castle

programme.title: Sort Of
programme.desc: As Sabi braces for their fathers return, Deenzie drops world-shaking news

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: Bookworm (2024)
programme.desc: Mildred, an 11-year-old girl, finds her world turned upside down when her estranged father, a washed-up magician, takes her on a wild adventure. Family comedy, starring Elijah Wood and Nell Fisher

programme.title: The Wild Robot (2024)
programme.desc: Following a shipwreck, a robot called Roz survives on an uninhabited island with the help of wild animals. Family animation, with the voices of Lupita Nyongo, Pedro Pascal, Kit Connor and Bill Nighy

programme.title: Babe (1995)
programme.desc: A kindly farmer wins a cute piglet, which some greedy humans and jealous animals hope will end up as the Christmas roast. Undaunted, the young pig proves himself useful on the farm and enlists the aid of the resident dog in learning how to herd sheep. Family comedy, starring James Cromwell and Magda Szubanski, with Christine Cavanaugh, Miriam Margolyes and Hugo Weaving among those providing the voices of the animal characters

programme.title: The Wizard of Oz (1939)
programme.desc: Youngster Dorothy Gale is whisked over the rainbow by a tornado into the magical land of Oz, where she inadvertently kills a wicked witch - incurring the wrath of another. Setting out to find the wizard who can show her a way home, she is joined on her journey by a scarecrow, a tin man and a cowardly lion, who all hope their own wishes will be granted. Musical fantasy, with Judy Garland, Ray Bolger, Jack Haley, Bert Lahr and Frank Morgan

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001)
programme.desc: An unassuming hobbit is plunged into a battle against the forces of darkness when his uncle bequeaths him a magical ring of immense power. He joins a fellowship of heroes to embark on a perilous quest to destroy the ring before it falls into the hands of its creator - a dark lord who needs it to regain his power and conquer the world. The first part of Peter Jacksons epic fantasy trilogy based on JRR Tolkiens novel, starring Elijah Wood, Ian McKellen, Viggo Mortensen, Sean Astin, Christopher Lee, Liv Tyler and Orlando Bloom

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers (2002)
programme.desc: Frodo and Sam continue their quest to destroy the all-powerful ring, bringing them face to face with the magical artefacts twisted former owner Gollum. Meanwhile, their allies prepare to make a desperate last stand against the vast armies of the corrupted wizard Saruman, who has declared war on a neighbouring kingdom. Part two of Peter Jacksons fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Sean Astin, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Billy Boyd, Dominic Monaghan, Andy Serkis and Christopher Lee

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King (2003)
programme.desc: As hobbits Frodo and Sam near the end of their journey into the dark lands of Mordor to destroy the One Ring, the evil Sauron unleashes the full might of his armies on Middle-earth. Meanwhile, Aragorn must fulfil his destiny of becoming king by leading his people into a climactic battle with the forces of darkness. Conclusion of the fantasy adventure trilogy, starring Elijah Wood, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen and Sean Astin

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside towns tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chiefs efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielbergs blockbusting thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw, with an Oscar-winning score by John Williams. The film also won Academy Awards for Best Sound and Best Film Editing

programme.title: Get Out (2017)
programme.desc: After African American photographer Chris and his white girlfriend Rose visit her wealthy parents, he suspects theres more to the locals than meets the eye. Oscar-winning psychological thriller written and directed by Jordan Peele (Us), starring Daniel Kaluuya, Allison Williams, Bradley Whitford and Caleb Landry Jones

programme.title: Weapons (2025)
programme.desc: At 2.17am, 17 children from the class of Justine Gandy run into the night, arms outstretched, never to be seen again. The only child from the class who remains safely in their bed is Alex Lilly. Distraught parent Archer Graff joins other members of the local community in pointing a finger of blame at Ms Gandy for the shocking turn of events. While she pleads innocence, school principal Andrew Marcus seeks answers along with police officer Paul Morgan. Horror, starring Julia Garner, Josh Brolin and Alden Ehrenreich

programme.title: 47 Metres Down (2017)
programme.desc: Two sisters vacationing in Mexico are trapped in a shark cage at the bottom of the ocean. With less than an hour of oxygen left and great white sharks circling nearby, they must fight to survive. Horror, starring Mandy Moore, Claire Holt and Matthew Modine

programme.title: Strange Darling (2023)
programme.desc: A twisted one-night stand devolves into a serial killers vicious killing spree and a cat-and-mouse chase. Writer/director JT Mollners thriller, starring Willa Fitzgerald, Kyle Gallner, Madisen Beaty and Barbara Hershey

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview - April 2026
programme.desc: Previews of the films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights

programme.title: Jaws (1975)

The local police chiefs efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielbergs blockbusting thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw, with an Oscar-winning score by John Williams. The film also won Academy Awards for Best Sound and Best Film Editing

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. While police chief Brody struggles to alert the townsfolk to the magnitude of the danger, a group of teenagers - including Brodys two sons - is adrift at sea in waters hunted by the shark. Director Jeannot Szwarcs thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton, with John Williams once more providing the acclaimed score

programme.title: The Secret of Marrowbone (2017)
programme.desc: A young man and his three siblings have kept the death of their beloved mother secret in order to remain together. However, they are plagued by a sinister presence in their sprawling manor. Writer/director Sergio G Sanchezs horror, starring George MacKay, Anya Taylor-Joy and Charlie Heaton

programme.title: Afraid (2024)
programme.desc: Computer engineering company executive Curtis is coerced into testing a new digital assistant named AIA to secure a lucrative business contract with two tech geniuses. At first, the omnipresent artificial intelligence simplifies day-to-day life. However, AIA becomes dangerously protective of the clan. Writer/director Chris Weitzs sci-fi horror, starring John Cho, Katherine Waterston and Keith Carradine

programme.title: The Watched (2024)
programme.desc: Mina, a 28-year old artist, gets stranded in an expansive, untouched forest in western Ireland. When Mina finds shelter, she unknowingly becomes trapped alongside three strangers that are stalked by mysterious creatures each night. Horror, starring Dakota Fanning, Georgina Campbell and Olwen Fouere

programme.title: Salems Lot (2024)
programme.desc: A writer attempts to destroy vampires in a small American town. Horror based on Stephen Kings novel of the same name, starring Lewis Pullman, Makenzie Leigh, Jordan Preston Carter and Alfre Woodard

programme.title: The Damned (2024)
programme.desc: A 19th-century widow must make an unimaginable choice when, during a cruel winter, a foreign ship sinks off the coast of her fishing village. Horror mystery, starring Odessa Young, Joe Cole, Siobhan Finneran and Rory McCann

programme.title: Out of Darkness (2022)
programme.desc: In the Stone Age, a gang of early humans face a mystical being hellbent on hunting them down. Prehistoric horror, starring Iola Evans and Arno Luning. The dialogue is in a specially contructed language and features subtitles

programme.desc: A group of soldiers get stranded on a raft in the middle of the ocean after their ship is sunk, and must battle a great white shark. Writer/director Kiah Roache-Turners horror thriller, starring Mark Coles Smith and Joel Nankervis

programme.title: The Toxic Avenger (2025)
programme.desc: Bob Garbinger is the money-oriented CEO of a factory that is pumping out dangerous chemicals into the close-knit community of St Romas Villa. Residents are poisoned by toxins, including widowed janitor Winston Gooze, who works at the factory and learns he has an incurable brain condition. Whistleblower JJ shares devastating information about the factorys reckless disregard for public safety and Winston undergoes a physical transformation in response to exposure to toxic ooze. He takes on the form of a hideously deformed vigilante and wreaks havoc on Bob and his brother Fritz. Comedy horror, starring Peter Dinklage, Kevin Bacon and Taylour Paige

programme.title: Bring Her Back (2025)
programme.desc: Seventeen-year-old Andy and his visually impaired younger stepsister Piper discover their father dead in the shower. Single mother Laura agrees to take in Andy and Piper until the traumatised youngster comes of age and can apply for legal guardianship of his stepsister. Hoever, when events inside Lauras home spiral out of control, Andy fears he is losing his mind. Thriller, starring Billy Barratt, Sora Wong and Sally Hawkins

programme.title: Regretting You (2025)
programme.desc: A mother and daughter grapple with whats left after a devastating accident reveals a shocking betrayal and forces them to rediscover themselves. Romantic comedy drama, starring Allison Williams, Mckenna Grace and Dave Franco

programme.desc: Former baseball player Henry Hank Thompson foolishly agrees to cat-sit for British neighbour Russ, and walks unwittingly into the middle of a turf war between warring factions. Director Darren Aronofskys comedy thriller, starring Austin Butler, Zoe Kravitz and Matt Smith

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2 (2025)
programme.desc: Struggling to adjust to their newly minted lives as Good Guys, the Bad Guys are offered one last job by a group of all-female criminals. Animated sequel, featuring the voices of Sam Rockwell, Marc Maron and Craig Robinson

programme.title: Superman (2025)
programme.desc: Kal-El, one of the last survivors of the planet Krypton, attempts to protect the people of Earth from evil genius Lex Luthor. Superman is assisted by the Justice Gang, comprising Guy Gardner, aka Green Lantern, Kendra Saunders, aka Hawkgirl, Michael Holt, aka Mister Terrific and Rex Mason, aka Metamorpho. Writer/director James Gunns fantasy adventure, starring David Corenswet, Rachel Brosnahan and Nicholas Hoult

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey (2025)
programme.desc: New York bachelor David is due to drive to his friends wedding, but a wheel clamp on his vehicle derails those plans. He acquires a new drive from the mysterious Car Rental Company managed by two nameless agents, who are oddly familiar with his dating history. At the wedding, David meets fellow singleton Sarah and squanders an opportunity to dance with her. On the way home, the rental cars GPS asks him if he would like to embark on a big bold beautiful journey. When David agrees, the virtual assistant guides him to Sarah and they follow a winding route between physical doors that open into episodes from their respective pasts. Romantic drama, starring Colin Farrell and Margot Robbie

Jeffrey hides out at a toy shop for several months, and during forays outside the building, he sparks romance with store worker Leigh Wainscott. programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: When the Lamb Funeral Homes crematorium is destroyed by a fire in 1986, an undeterred David Sconce finds ways to expand the family business. As allegations of abuse mount, he is eventually arrested and the ensuing police investigation uncovers shocking evidence that points to Sconce being possibly connected to the assault and later death of a rival mortician

programme.title: The Mortician
programme.desc: After members of the Lamb family are convicted of fraud and various other crimes, a murder charge is brought against David Sconce for the death of his rival, but the case is dropped due to a lack of hard evidence. With more than 20,000 families affected by the practices at the Lamb Funeral Home, the case brings about reforms and new regulations to the mortuary business

programme.title: Touching the Void (2003)

The story is told in the climbers own words and features dramatised reconstructions of their ordeal starring Brendan Mackey and Nicholas Aaron

programme.title: Murder at the Cottage
programme.desc: Jim Sheridan talks to a key witness who called the Irish police back in 1996 with information about Sophie Toscan du Plantiers murder

programme.title: Murder at the Cottage
programme.desc: False leads muddy the investigation into Sophies murder. Despite having more than 50 initial suspects, no charges are made

programme.title: Hitlers British Island
programme.desc: Part one of two. How the Channel Island of Alderney became a pre-occupation of Nazi leader Adolf Hitler and under German control was transformed into one of the most heavily fortified places on Earth. Away from prying eyes, the Nazis also established a concentration camp - the only one on British soil - amid the idyllic scenery of woodlands and silver sand beaches

programme.title: Hitlers British Island
programme.desc: Part two of two. On the orders of Adolf Hitler, the Nazi invaders of Alderney, a tiny island in the English Channel, shipped in more than 5,000 slave labourers to build an astonishing series of fortifications designed to protect the island from attack

programme.title: Combat Ships
programme.desc: The crucial role ships have played in international conflict over the past 500 years, beginning with the battleships declining influence during the 20th century

programme.sub-title: The Lost Standard, the Steam Gun and the Sacrificial Throne
programme.desc: This latest collection of objects comprises an intriguing 35,000-year-old box, a steam-powered gun and a monument that may have been an Aztec throne

programme.title: Ancient Superstructures
programme.desc: The ancient city of Chichen Itza in Mexico, exploring the construction and purpose of ancient monuments, and how they were built without modern technology

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A countdown of the top 10 alien petroglyphs around the world, from strange figures to flying craft and carvings of what appear to be astronauts

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The life of self-proclaimed faith healer and psychic Edgar Cayce. He became a worldwide sensation in the early 1900s, claiming to receive visions of both the past and future

programme.title: Historys Greatest Aircraft
programme.desc: The Hawker Hurricane was the first RAF aircraft to fly over 300mph. Over half of every enemy aeroplane destroyed in the Battle of Britain was by a Hurricane, not a Spitfire

programme.title: Hitlers Engineers
programme.desc: How the designer of one of the most popular car brands in history, Ferdinand Porsche, would not have seen his success without the support of Adolf Hitler

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team follows the search for artefacts from the Second World War, joining marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. Elsewhere, a dig in the Lake District tells the moving story of the Windermere Boys, and in Aldbourne in Wiltshire, archaeologists search for personal items from the most famous American unit of the US army, Easy Company, who were stationed there in 1943 and 1944

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits a secret location in the Cotswolds, which appears to be a high-status Anglo Saxon cemetery, where she finds a very precious and fragile artefact. A team from Cardiff University is called to the bone cave of Wales to investigate medieval bones protruding from a cliff face on the Welsh coast

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The actor is joined by advanced technical diver Emily Turton in north Devon, as he prepares to dive down on the wreck of a sunken steam ship. Iona II was heading to America in the early 1860s, when it went down with a mystery cargo on board that the owners went to extensive efforts to recover in secret. The pair embark on a night dive with the hope it will reveal what the owners so desperately tried to covertly retrieve. Determined to get to the bottom of the mystery, Ross also meets with a maritime historian who has uncovered Iona IIs shocking purpose. Ross and maritime archaeologist Mallory Haas head to Plymouth to dive on a newly discovered wreck, and to uncover a staggering artefact that links the site to one of the most controversial and censored periods in British history - the transatlantic slave trade

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross is joined by maritime archaeologist Mallory Haas in Plymouth to deep dive on the wreck site of 36-gun Nelsonian frigate HMS Amethyst - a legendary battle cruiser, launched in 1799, which proved one of the most successful and prolific of the Napoleonic wars. Ross makes an incredible discovery using an underwater metal detector, and the historic item reveals why the ship was such a potent weapon of war, but also gives clues as to how and why it sank

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A countdown of the top 10 alien petroglyphs around the world, from strange figures to flying craft and carvings of what appear to be astronauts

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: The life of self-proclaimed faith healer and psychic Edgar Cayce. He became a worldwide sensation in the early 1900s, claiming to receive visions of both the past and future

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In Guatemala, an old site made up of a series of strange structures is found. On a remote island, a lake full of jellyfish is studied. Argentinian researchers find a complex that may have a dark past, while a flower found in Sumatra gives off a foul scent

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: Archaeologists find an ancient structure built on granite columns, puzzling stone spheres are found in Costa Rica and Mexican skeletal remains emerge

programme.title: The Jewish Nazi?
programme.desc: The story of Alex Kurzem, a young Jewish boy who survived the massacre of his Belarusian shtetl during WWII, only to be taken in by the Nazi SS. He witnessed the massacre of his family and entire community by an execution squad. Escaping into the Belarusian woods, he braved a brutal winter alone until his capture by a Latvian battalion that had been absorbed into the SS. Instead of killing him, the soldiers took pity on Alex and gave him a uniform, rifle, and a new name-transforming him into their child mascot

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Search for King Solomons Mines
programme.desc: Fascinating new evidence reveals fresh clues about the whereabouts of King Solomons mines - and the fabulous treasures said to be in them. Narrated by Jamie Theakston

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: Recounting how, as the war encompassed the globe, the conflict took root in Africa. Mussolini sent ill-prepared soldiers into the field, forcing Germany to come to his aid

programme.title: World War II: Witness to War
programme.desc: The US military faced a formidable enemy in Japans forces at sea and in the air. Here, the men and women who fought in the Pacific share their stories

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: A countdown of the top 10 alien petroglyphs around the world, from strange figures to flying craft and carvings of what appear to be astronauts

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts question whether the facts of Earths alien past could be buried in areas that - thanks to warfare or instability - are presently off-limits to exploration

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: The unexpected things revealed by catastrophic disasters - both natural and human made - can reveal in their wake. In this episode, fish remains found near an angry Sumatran volcano might suggest a new human migration route and a collapsed kingdom in Turkey is discovered

programme.title: Discovered by Disaster
programme.desc: The team members dig into artefacts from a Jamaican port sunk by a devastating earthquake. As they progress, they take a peek at a Minoan landmark and inspect a treasure trove of English relics

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: Craig and co-presenter Sarah Cruddas head to central Scotland, which has its own Area 51, known as the Falkirk Triangle, encompassing the now infamous village of Bonnybridge, which claims the highest concentration of the regions UFO activity. They also investigate the alleged abduction of forestry worker Robert Taylor in 1979, the only British UFO incident to have been officially investigated by the police

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Experts question who the Anti-Christ is, considering that every culture and religion on the planet believes that an evil one will usher in the end of days

programme.title: Conspiracy Files: Unsealed
programme.desc: Questioning whether technology is going too far, too fast, considering that people rely on machines for virtually every aspect of modern life

programme.title: Blown Away
programme.desc: Ten master artists turn up the heat in glassblowing sculpture challenges for the chance to win $60,000 in prizes and the title of champion. The competitors look to mirrors for inspiration as they create pieces that represent how they see themselves

programme.title: Blown Away
programme.desc: In the second challenge, the nine remaining glassblowers must choose a mystery box and use only the colours inside to make a functional household item. Their work will be evaluated based on design concept, execution and functionality. Items include a cookie jar, a candelabrum and one very sophisticated sippy cup

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Specialists in the field of the extraterrestrial study evidence across 75 million years to validate the theory that there has been extraterrestrial intervention on Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts question whether the facts of Earths alien past could be buried in areas that - thanks to warfare or instability - are presently off-limits to exploration

programme.title: Hell on Earth: WWII
programme.desc: The documentary charts the conflict from the Indian border in Burma to China, at war with Japan since 1937, as well as Hitlers renewed assault on the Soviet Union

programme.title: Lost U-Boats of WWII
programme.desc: During the Second World War, the Nazis looted an estimated 600 tons of gold, thousands of pieces of artwork, and millions of priceless artifacts. While some of these items have been found, much of it remains missing. Thanks to top-secret documents acquired over 40 years of research, treasure hunter Darrell Miklos believes some of these stolen riches were loaded into specially modified U-Boats that are currently lying at the bottom of the Caribbean Sea. He and his team of divers and researchers set out to find these lost vessels

programme.title: How to Lose a War
programme.sub-title: The Men Who Gave the Orders
programme.desc: Strategic catastrophes and doomed military decisions, beginning by looking at those in charge whose rampant egos and misguided belief in their own abilities led to their country making fatal mistakes in the war.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Experts question whether the facts of Earths alien past could be buried in areas that - thanks to warfare or instability - are presently off-limits to exploration

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts seeks out the most outstanding archaeological digs of the year, beginning in the east of England. She witnesses the uncovering of a Roman mosaic found on farmland in Rutland, and visits the site of a saintly hermits hovel discovered in the Lincolnshire fens

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts heads to the south of England to see the rich pickings on offer, including the earliest money made in Britain and a massive haul of Roman treasure that brings to life the fall of the empire in Britain

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits the north of Britain to look at the most fascinating archaeology uncovered in the region over the course of 2021. In North Yorkshire, a team of community volunteers are digging down beneath Richmond Castle. On the northern tip of the Orkney Islands, the dig diary cameras are present to witness archaeologists racing to unearth rare discoveries from an endangered Neolithic tomb before it erodes into the sea. And 10 miles east of Edinburgh, volunteers are hunting for evidence of Scotlands oldest railway

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Just outside Leicester, Alice joins a team of archaeologists who are investigating a mysterious mound thought to be an Iron Age hillfort, but what they unearth tells a rich story of bandits, religious crusades and connections across the medieval world. In Cambridge, the dig diary cameras are present to witness archaeologists unearthing an extraordinary array of exquisite artefacts from an early Anglo-Saxon cemetery

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists in Somerset are blown away by the discovery of an entirely new Roman town in the intended pathway of some new electricity cables. Multiple, impressive layers of road building from the Roman era are revealed, in particular a spectacular section from the fourth century when Emperor Constantine presided over a boom in Britains economy. Elsewhere in Pembrokeshire, Alice Roberts revisits Whitesands Bay where a team are excavating a mysterious collection of child burials

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Infrared UFO Attack, Human Bear and Underwater Crop Circles
programme.desc: An examination of several seemingly impossible things caught on film, including claims a trucker captured a legendary Native American creature on his dashcam and the suggestion that underwater extraterrestrials might be leaving messages. Plus, the UFO that apparently followed two researchers home from an investigation and the bear who successfully learned to act like a human

programme.title: Lost U-Boats of WWII
programme.desc: During the Second World War, the Nazis looted an estimated 600 tons of gold, thousands of pieces of artwork, and millions of priceless artifacts. While some of these items have been found, much of it remains missing. programme.title: How to Lose a War
programme.sub-title: The Men Who Gave the Orders
programme.desc: Strategic catastrophes and doomed military decisions, beginning by looking at those in charge whose rampant egos and misguided belief in their own abilities led to their country making fatal mistakes in the war.

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists visit the most excavated monastic site in Europe, tucked away in the corner of an industrial park in Runcorn, Cheshire. An investigation into the bones of the people buried here reveals a story of medieval knights, brutal murder, and generations of people afflicted by an ancient disease

programme.title: Cracking the Code
programme.desc: A look at the roller-coaster ride that cracking the human genome entailed, humanitys most complex and important code. The bid pushes modern science to its very limits

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Specialists in the field of the extraterrestrial study evidence across 75 million years to validate the theory that there has been extraterrestrial intervention on Earth

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Theorists speculate about metal artifacts that could provide physical evidence of extraterrestrials visiting Earth in the distant past. In Jacksonville, Florida, a strange metallic sphere falls from the sky, while an ancient aluminum wedge also baffles historians as to its source

programme.title: Alien Files Reopened
programme.sub-title: Billy Meier, The Plejaren and the Multiverse, 1975
programme.desc: In 1975, Swiss national Billy Meier claimed he had travelled on a beamship through space with extraterrestrial friends. He stated he had been in contact with these extraterrestrials telepathically since the age of five and that they allowed him to take photographs and videos of their spacecraft

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis is joined by a team of expert archaeologists to excavate back gardens around Britain, uncovering the lost history buried beneath our lawns and flower beds. In the first episode, Hugh and the team attempt to uncover a Roman fort, connected to Hadrians Wall, that is buried beneath the back gardens of the quiet suburban streets of Benwell in Newcastle-upon-Tyne

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts seeks out the most outstanding archaeological digs of the year, beginning in the east of England. She witnesses the uncovering of a Roman mosaic found on farmland in Rutland, and visits the site of a saintly hermits hovel discovered in the Lincolnshire fens

programme.title: Mysteries from Above
programme.desc: A look at colossal wonders scattered across the Earths surface, including an ancient reptile skeleton, a massive head carving in the Alberta badlands, a giant South African footprint, and a pentagram etched into a remote peninsula in Kazakhstan

programme.title: Historys Deadliest with Ving Rhames
programme.desc: Reliving historys deadliest battles, from Cannae and Agincourt to Stalingrad and the Battle of the Bulge

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. In the first edition, five bodies dating from the Bronze Age are found in a deep pit on the Isle of Thanet in Kent. One of them is an old woman, who has been manipulated into a position where she is pointing at something. But at what? And why?

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Leeds, where remains of children and teenagers have been uncovered beneath a shopping arcade. Analysis of the broken bones reveals that they date back to the industrial revolution, and a thorough examination of the remains provides a detailed picture of the lives these young people would have lived during a tumultuous period of British history

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets
programme.desc: Tori Herridge and a team of scientists attempt to piece together the lives behind discovered bones, and find out what happened to these people and how they died. This episode comes from Andover, Hampshire, where the construction of a proposed car park outside the city has been held up by the discovery of remains from over 100 young men. The bones are discovered to date back to Anglo-Saxon times, but appear to have been left in a mass grave rather than granted the Christian burial that was normal for their era

programme.title: Bone Detectives: Britains Buried Secrets

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of six of Britains most famous castles, his journey commencing with a visit to Edinburgh Castle. A popular tourist attraction to this day, Dan explores the history of this famous stronghold that began as an Iron Age battle base and remains a military facility to this day, and was the target of the worlds first aerial bombing

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of the Welsh capitals landmark, Cardiff Castle, and discovers more about its various incarnations over the centuries. Dan learns about the many famous figures throughout history who have spent time within its walls, and discovers more about its redevelopment in the 19th century by architect William Burges, who transformed it into the Victorian gothic fantasy it remains today

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice

programme.title: Secrets in the Ice
programme.desc: A Kazakh farmer discovers ancient burial sites that may determine whether or not the legend of the Amazon women warriors is myth or reality. programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the north Norfolk coast through one of the least developed spots in Britain, finding out how the area maintained its rural, remote identity despite the dynamic industrial changes of the Victorian age. From the arrival of Bertie, Prince of Wales at Sandringham House to the opening of Cromer Pier, Tonys four-day walk sees him learn about key periods in the countys history. He explores prime shooting estates, purpose-built seaside resorts, salt marshes and disused railway lines as he discovers how powerful landowners were able to control development and even shape the railways to suit their needs

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britains most historic landscapes. The moors and valleys of West Yorkshire were the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontës, the literary family who produced such classics as Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre. Over four days, Tony heads out from the Victorian wool capital of Bradford and treks in a giant loop around what is now known as Brontë Country. From the birth of Charlotte, Emily, Anne and their brother Branwell in the suburb of Thornton, he traces their childhoods to the much-romanticised Brontë hub of Haworth, finding that the harsh reality of life in the 1820s was far removed from the fantasy world dreamed up by the children at their fathers parsonage home

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Rise of Animals
programme.sub-title: From the Seas to the Skies
programme.desc: Part one of two. The naturalist explores the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years, beginning with a look at how remarkable advances allowed primitive fish to make the radical move on to land, and then take to the skies with the advent of flight. He uses fossils of both ancient and living species combined with CGI and film to chart their journey out of the water to populate all corners of the globe

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Rise of Animals
programme.desc: The naturalist concludes his two-part exploration of the evolution of vertebrates over the past 500 million years by looking at the history of mammals. He traces their journey from humble origins as tiny nocturnal forest-dwellers to their astonishing explosion in diversity and domination of the world following the death of the dinosaurs

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist examines the world of flight, travelling across the globe to chart the evolutionary story of those creatures who seemingly defy the rules of gravity by taking to the air. Initially focusing on insects, Attenborough meets a descendant of the dragonfly, before examining the Atlas beetle, which tends to restrict flight to times when it needs a mate or a fresh supply of food. More aesthetically pleasing is a group of beautiful tropical butterflies, followed by an in-depth study into the blow fly - the jet fighter of insects

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist visits the jungles of Borneo to meet creatures that like to glide through life - the harlequin tree frog and the little-known colugo - often referred to, incorrectly, as a flying lemur. He also has a CGI-assisted encounter with the first true vertebrate flyer, the pterosaur, receives a flying lesson from a small flock of swans at Loch Lomond in Scotland and encounters the planets largest species of bat

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The veteran naturalist turns his attention to creatures that dominate the skies today. He begins with the vast bird family, watching a group of vultures as they go scavenging and some peregrine falcons dive bombing for their prey. He then moves on to the floating forests of Ecuador to find out what makes the humble hummingbird tick, before ending his journey at the Gomantong bat caves of Borneo - a place he first filmed in 1972 - witnessing a mass exodus of the creatures

programme.title: David Attenboroughs Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: The making of David Attenboroughs documentary series charting the evolution of creatures that defy the rules of gravity

programme.title: Americas Wild Border: Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The animals that live on the border between the US and Canada face many challenges - even politics. This documentary reveals how the boundary affects them over 12 months

programme.title: Wild Philippines
programme.desc: Cameras follow turtles and tiger sharks roaming volcanic reefs, and tarsiers leaping through forests on the hunt

programme.title: Arabian Seas
programme.desc: A look at the marine wildlife in the waters off the Arabian Peninsula, beginning with sharks and rays that patrol Arabias coral reefs for their prey, and the toxic fish that have perfected the art of disguise

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: A look at evolutionary changes that appear invisible to the naked eye, yet the effect of which can be massive

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: The question of sight can be one of life or death in the animal world. See how some creatures have evolved to see perfectly while others can be nearly invisible

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: The importance of cooperation in the animal kingdom, where some creatures work together to be social while others put the welfare of the group above their own potential

programme.title: Mysteries of Evolution
programme.desc: From the shark to the horseshoe crab, meet the planets living fossils - the creatures that have survived relatively unchanged for millions of years

programme.title: Patrick Aryees Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick takes a monumental adventure that explores the breathtaking wild world, beginning by asking whether peoples appetite for salmon is wiping out the lynx

programme.title: Patrick Aryees Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick travels to Yellowstone National Park, where he discovers the positive, top-down domino effect that wolves have on their environment

programme.title: Patrick Aryees Wild World
programme.desc: Patrick is in Costa Rica to witness the annual mass nesting of sea turtles, as well as the predatory jaguars that come in for the kill

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The Bay of Fundy on Canadas east coast, which is home to the worlds highest tides - beneath which lies an intricate maze of invertebrate life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Dr Alison Cronin and animal manager Jeremy Keeling return from Argentina with chimpanzees Sasha and Kangoo, and Tuans orang-utans receive a treat

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Wild Dogs: Pack vs Pride
programme.desc: The Kakumbi mourn the loss of two of their pups and the team witness a standoff between a leopard and a herd of angry elephants

programme.title: Men in Black (1997)
programme.desc: A streetwise cop is enlisted by a top-secret government force dedicated to policing alien visitors to Earth, teaming up with a veteran agent just as a body-swapping extraterrestrial giant insect begins a reign of terror throughout New York City. Sci-fi comedy, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Linda Fiorentino, Rip Torn and Vincent DOnofrio

programme.title: Men in Black II (2002)
programme.desc: Agent J investigates the unauthorised landing of a spacecraft and discovers a malevolent alien disguised as a lingerie model is on the verge of destroying humanity. His only hope of stopping her diabolical plot is to restore the memory of his retired partner K - but his former mentor takes more than a little convincing. Sci-fi comedy sequel, directed by Barry Sonnenfeld, starring Will Smith, Tommy Lee Jones, Rosario Dawson, Lara Flynn Boyle, Johnny Knoxville, Rip Torn and Tony Shalhoub

programme.title: Men in Black 3 (2012)
programme.desc: An alien criminal escapes from a prison on the moon and goes back in time on a mission of revenge, changing the course of history. Agent J of the top-secret Earth defence organisation follows him back to 1969, and joins forces with a younger version of his mentor Agent K to set things right. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Will Smith, Josh Brolin and Tommy Lee Jones

programme.title: Warehouse 13
programme.sub-title: All the Time in the World
programme.desc: The team tries to track down Paracelsus, who has turned Claudia into a bronze statue and escaped with the only artefact that can cure her. Claudias deteriorating condition apparently affects both Mrs Frederic and the Warehouse. Sci-fi fantasy, starring Eddie McClintock, Joanne Kelly and Anthony Head

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Carter takes a brilliant, if undisciplined, cadet under her wing, hoping to motivate her with a trip through the Stargate. However, she has cause to regret her decision when the pair are attacked by alien life forms made of pure energy at an off-world research site. Elisabeth Rosen guest stars, with Amanda Tapping and Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: Chakotays apparent attempts to instigate a mutiny turn out to be nothing more than an unfinished holographic training exercise, but further investigations show the programme has been laced with traps by the villainous Seska

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: A Ferengi whose child Picard killed in battle several years previously, reveals he plans to avenge the death by murdering the Enterprise captains son. The team is faced with locating and protecting the boy - a task which proves more difficult when he contracts a mystery illness. Patrick Stewart stars

programme.title: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
programme.desc: Bashir and OBrien lure Sloan back to the station in a bid to discover the cure for Odos degenerative disease, but the shady agent has one more trick up his sleeve. Starring Avery Brooks, Alexander Siddig and Colm Meaney

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Carter takes a brilliant, if undisciplined, cadet under her wing, hoping to motivate her with a trip through the Stargate. Elisabeth Rosen guest stars, with Amanda Tapping and Richard Dean Anderson

programme.title: From
programme.desc: The return of the sci-fi horror about a nightmarish town that traps everyone who enters. In Sheriff Boyds absence Donna and Kenny struggle to manage the chaos as a busload of unwitting newcomers arrives

programme.title: From
programme.desc: Its a tense night in the diner as the residents wait for sunrise. Jim and Tom struggle to keep a panicked bus passenger quiet

programme.title: The Walking Dead
programme.desc: The Governor throws a party with a unique twist for the people of Woodbury, but Michonne remains suspicious of his motives and carries out some covert investigations. Meanwhile, another loss leaves Rick facing a crisis, and Daryl and Maggie go scavenging for supplies after Hershel tells the group that Loris baby needs infant formula to survive. Post-apocalyptic drama, starring David Morrissey

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Juliette regains the last of her memories and wants to get back together with Nick, whose latest case uncovers a plot to create an army of zombies. Adalind makes a dangerous bargain with Stefania to regain her supernatural powers - in exchange for her unborn child

programme.desc: In March 1973, 18-year-old Marian Beattie from Portadown went to a charity dance at Haddens Garage on the outskirts of Aughnacloy, Co Tyrone, with her best friend and brother. During the night she was seen dancing with a young man. Minutes after leaving with him, she was murdered. Her body was found at the bottom of a nearby quarry. This programme explores Marians familys efforts to get to the truth of what happened to their sister that night and why after 50 years, the murder of an innocent 18-year-old woman remains unsolved

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldons flat is burgled, so they work on a state-of-the-art security system in a bid to outwit any future would-be thieves. However, their plans go awry when they ensnare Penny in a net. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a mental block and has difficulty working on a formula, so in a move inspired by Albert Einstein he gets a job at the Cheesecake Factory diner to try to revive his creativity - and despite Pennys objections, proves surprisingly effective in the role. Guest starring Yeardley Smith (The voice of Lisa Simpson)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to visit the Large Hadron Collider in Switzerland and asks Penny to join him as a Valentines Day treat. However, Sheldon tries to persuade the couple to allow him to take her place

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected

programme.title: Mike and Molly
programme.desc: Mike is delighted when he hits a personal best weight-loss goal, but then he makes the risky decision to indulge in a slice of pie. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike and Molly
programme.desc: Molly is nervous about Mike attending a literary party that her publisher is throwing, but his feelings are hurt when he realises she doesnt want him to go. She reluctantly brings her family, but Mike is left to hang out with a human statue. Comedy, starring Billy Gardell and Melissa McCarthy

programme.title: Mike and Molly
programme.desc: Mike and Vince bond while fixing a water heater, and Peggy goes back to her home town in rural Missouri as part of Mollys research for her new book

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Dolomite, Apple Slices and a Mystery Woman
programme.desc: Sheldon makes friends with an upper-class senior pupil who introduces him to a new field of science. Meanwhile, Mary and George disagree on how to handle Sheldons blossoming social life

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Killer Asteroids, Oklahoma and a Frizzy Hair Machine
programme.desc: A disappointing lack of success in a competition at the school science fair sends Sheldons life in a new direction as he rejects the idea of becoming a scientist in favour of acting. Comedy, starring Iain Armitage

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: When Sheldon is bullied by an unlikely character, Mary and George disagree on how to handle the situation, contemplating whether they should their son employ self-defence techniques. Undeterred, he tries his hand at jiu-jitsu and even covers himself in bubble-wrap for protection

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The friends fight over a ring they believe was used in the Lord of the Rings film trilogy. Leonard hopes to return the item to its rightful owner - but Raj wishes to sell it so he can buy a jet ski, and Sheldon develops a Gollum-like obsession with the precious object

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is awarded a prestigious prize by the university, but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows hell have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee him up with rousing references to X-Men, and Leonard and Raj offer psychological guidance - without much success

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny he loves her, but fails to get the response he would have liked. Sheldon also finds himself competing against Star Trek: The Next Generation actor Wil Wheaton once again when the gang goes bowling - and tries to get his revenge

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to hide the fact he had dinner with Penny after she broke up with Leonard, hoping to stay friends with her. However, keeping secrets does not prove to be his strong point, and it isnt long before his deceit is exposed. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Kaley Cuoco and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon invites a famous physicist to the flat after befriending her online, but is put out to find Leonard showing an interest in her. Meanwhile, Penny has to confront her ex-boyfriend when the visitor turns up in his bedroom. Guest starring Judy Greer (Glenn Martin, DDS)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The comedy flashes back to reveal how Sheldon saved the lives of his pals, and looks at the origins of his friendship with Leonard seven years previously, as well as explaining the housemates agreement. Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj and Howard register Sheldon with an online dating site hoping to find his perfect female equivalent. Meanwhile, a drunk Penny seduces Leonard, but regrets it the next day. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco. Mayim Bialik makes her first appearance on the show

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny is stunned when Sheldon announces he is about to go on his first-ever date, and is even more surprised to find herself tagging along. Meanwhile, Leonard and Raj take a trip to the hospital after Howards new robotic arm creates problems

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara Ó Briain hosts topical chat and hilarious games with guests Rhys James, Hugh Dennis, Katherine Ryan, Sara Pascoe, Glenn Moore and Sarah Keyworth

programme.title: Zero Stars
programme.desc: Roisin Conaty and Sara Pascoe adventure across Albania, from target practice in the city to an Elvis taxi to the coast and suspicious yoghurt drinks in the country.

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Gerald returns to see D. Lee and a hand specialist about a huge, intrusive bump on his finger. Vinnie has an embarrassing birthmark on his neck.

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Jess is looking for love but is stuck with a giant bump on his neck, and former NFL defensive tackle, Marcell Dareus, hopes Dr. Lee can tackle his forehead lipoma

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Nhan has an extreme, red birthmark that covers half his face, while Debbie worries that her shoulder lump will prematurely age her

programme.title: My 600lb Life
programme.desc: Addiction counsellor Bethany has got so big that she can no longer work. Now she risks losing her life if Dr Now cannot help her curb her own addiction

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress
programme.sub-title: Not Afraid to Try New Things
programme.desc: Excited wedding planner Rachel tries on some edgy dresses. Natalie wants a unique gown, but knows her traditionally minded mother may not approve. Kleinfeld employee Subrina hopes her customised dress will match her vision

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress
programme.sub-title: Im a Firm Believer in Signs
programme.desc: An American football player helps his sister choose a dress, and a bride wants a gown that compliments her personal tribute to her dead father

programme.title: Sex Clinic (1972)
programme.desc: A nurse runs a clinic that secretly provides sexual services for people with unusual kinks - but she also blackmails her unwitting clients. Her carefree life is complicated by her relationship with another woman and an unexpected run-in with her estranged son. Drama, starring Georgina Ward and Alexander Davion

programme.title: Whats Good for the Goose (1969)
programme.desc: An ageing bank managers life turns upside down during a company conference where he falls in love. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom, Sally Geeson and Terence Alexander

programme.title: Futtocks End (1970)
programme.desc: A general invites a motley group of guests to stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Silent comedy. starring Ronnie Barker, Michael Hordern and Roger Livesey

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.sub-title: Revisited: Shoreham on Sea, Powys and Stockport
programme.desc: Five years ago, the team turned Ian and Ruths rural plot in Powys into a country garden in what was one of the series most emotional reveals. Charlie Dimmock returns to discover the garden looking better than ever and see how Ian and his sons have been busy with new projects. Plus, a revisit to a garden in Shoreham, West Sussex, that went from mediocre to a Mediterranean marvel and a garden that turned a Stockport couple into lord and lady of the manor

programme.title: Becoming Jazzy Whipps
programme.desc: Jazzy Whipps is the UKs biggest deaf influencer - but success brings trolling. It takes courage and resilience to be Jazzy. Funded and supported by Together TV and BSLBT.

programme.title: My Greek Kitchen
programme.desc: Tonia Buxton travels to Athens, where she rustles up Avgolemono soup - a filling chicken and rice recipe - and souvlaki, one of the Greeks favourite fast-food dishes

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: With Christmas around the corner in South Ayrshire, a deep freeze descends on Johns farm, creating havoc with the machinery and forcing him to cancel his glamping pod guests. On top of all that, imports of foreign lamb threaten profit margins for his lamb fattening business. In Moray, Nikki and Ollie pick up a big order from their local butcher. Theyre showcasing and selling their buffalo meat and other home-grown produce at a Christmas farmers market

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As a new year begins, Carianne and her brother-in-law Alexander take Faillish the bull to the Oban Highland Show, hoping to bag a rosette and sell him for a good price. In Inverkip, the time has come for Rachel and Camy to scan their ewes to see how many are pregnant - a big moment of truth for their fledgling breeding programme - and in South Ayrshire, the Barclays are up early to start lambing

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Inverkip, after a successful pregnancy scan, Rachel and Camy are getting their sheds ready for this years crop of Park Cheviot Ewes - the best in their flock. Nikki and Ollie welcome their first colony of rabbit kits as their breeding programme takes off. In the Cairngorms National Park, Robert heads to market to sell last years crop of spring calves - a nerve-racking wait to find out what this years profit for a years hard work will be

programme.title: Heartbreakers (2001)
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Max and Page develop a canny system of conning rich bachelors out of a fortune. Max marries the target, then Page seduces him, allowing for a lucrative divorce settlement. However, the scheme is threatened when Page falls in love, while a former victim tracks Max down hoping to give their marriage another chance. Comedy, starring Sigourney Weaver, Jennifer Love Hewitt, Gene Hackman, Ray Liotta and Jason Lee.

Drama, starring Maggie Stride and Gay Singleton

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: David and Alice have their hands full with toddler twins Beatrix and Martha. They moved in a year and a half ago, but have not had the time or energy to devote to their new garden. Low maintenance is key for any busy family, and the couple would like advice on how best to maintain the mature hedging which surrounds the garden. The old deck is rotten and slippery and theres an unusual fence separating the garden from a garden room used by the previous owners as a separate annexe. Theyve got £5,500 for designers Charlie Dimmock and Chris Hull to bring both parts of the garden back together as a family garden in which the twins can play whilst David and Alice make the most of the evening sun at the back of the garden

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill are in Cardiff to create a Californian-style garden for Ryan and Eve, drawing inspiration from their honeymoon

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: As the makeover show returns, Alan Titchmarsh and the team head to Fareham near Southampton to surprise Betty Richards, a life-long volunteer. The 90 year old suffers from macular degeneration, and her garden is a green-free concrete box - with so many levels and trip hazards that its off limits to the sight-impaired dare-devil fundraiser, so the team sets about transforming it into a safe haven

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: The chef travels across the country to discover the secrets of planting, rearing livestock and growing vegetables to help him transform his smallholding. He begins by bringing in new livestock and his first addition are pigs, but he needs to choose animals that will provide him with the best possible meat

programme.title: The Wild Gardener
programme.desc: Colin Stafford-Johnsons garden is now bursting with life, and with red squirrels a regular visitor to the tree tops, he spots signs of another, even more elusive, mammal. Could they be about to become neighbours? The native plants in his meadow begins to take hold, attracting a whole new host of visitors as clouds of insects move in

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill are in Cardiff to create a Californian-style garden for Ryan and Eve, drawing inspiration from their honeymoon

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: South African-born Linda, her husband Mark and Coco the cat share a one-bedroom floating home on Taggs Island in Hampton, Surrey. Its an idyllic location, but their home is also their workplace and its starting to feel a bit cramped. The couple want to stay put in their exclusive location, so Linda has hatched an ambitious plan to buy the boat next door and do it up all by herself. Its a huge challenge and it remains to be seen whether enthusiasm and passion will be enough to get the job done

programme.title: Marcus Wareings Tales from a Kitchen Garden
programme.desc: The chef travels across the country to discover the secrets of planting, rearing livestock and growing vegetables to help him transform his smallholding. programme.title: Queer Lives Today
programme.desc: Anna Mouzouris touching documentary following the aquatic escapades of two queer mermaids as they find belonging through mermaid races and mermaid conventions
