programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill travel to Trafford, Greater Manchester, to meet Hannah and Christie. The couple feel their boxy 42-square-metre garden doesnt reflect their fun-loving lifestyle. Chris is a DJ and Hannah plays the sax, and they believe the feel of a music festival would be a great theme for the team to incorporate into their plot

programme.title: Queer Lives Today
programme.desc: Shiva Raichandanis film follows Asifa as she prepares to undergo gender affirmation surgery, and the trans activist reflects on how her life has changed during her transition

programme.title: How to Have a Better Orgasm
programme.desc: Seven women give intimate interviews about their diverse experience of orgasms, sharing a variety of stories and techniques covering a range of ages. Personal accounts reveal that, across the board, theres a turning point in understanding orgasms and learning how to have a better one. For Helen, it was her first experience of lesbian sex, which made her realise how important communication is to sex, while Sam attributes it to meeting her husband, because it was the first time someone cared about her pleasure

programme.title: Adventures of a Plumbers Mate (1978)
programme.desc: An amorous plumber runs up against assorted crooks and low-lifes while searching for a golden toilet seat. Sex comedy, starring Christopher Neil, Arthur Mullard and Elaine Paige

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: AJ Odudu hosts as collectors bid for a pinball machine signed by Elton John, a fork liberated from Saddam Husseins palace and a prototype mid-century chair brought in by two sisters who have different feelings about it but are equally desperate to sell it. Plus a 1940s fly-o-plane fairground attraction that was once used to train fighter pilots

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: AJ Odudu hosts as buyers bid for the neon sign of a Soho sex shop and a piece of the wedding cake of William and Catherine, the Prince and Princess of Wales. Plus, one of only five remaining copies of Henry Becks redesign of the London Tube map dating back to the 1930s

programme.title: Flipping Profit

programme.desc: Murdoch investigates when a string of vicious animal attacks in High Park terrorise the residents of Toronto.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicias lavish New Year ball is ruined when a VIP guest is murdered. Drama based on the stories of GK Chesterton, with Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A birdwatcher is stabbed to death with his own knife, a case that holds a certain fascination for Humphrey, who was a keen twitcher in his youth. The victim was one of a group of enthusiasts who had visited the island for a glimpse of a famous parrot - but the team soon faces a conundrum as all the suspects were in full sight of one another when the crime was committed. As the cops search for answers, they uncover evidence of long-standing rivalries, secret jealousies and romantic entanglements. But would any of it justify killing a harmless ornithologist?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.sub-title: The Man with the Golden Gun
programme.desc: Humphrey and his colleagues are called to a neighbouring private island whose property developer owner has been shot dead at a family gathering. Five people are in the frame - the victims housekeeper, his PA and his three children - although when an approaching hurricane strands the cops on the island, knocking out the phones and computers, they are forced to rely on good old-fashioned investigative techniques. As evening approaches, the cops realise they will be spending the night in a house with a killer. Can they solve the mystery before he or she strikes again? Jimmy Akingbola (Holbys Antoine Malick) and Nikki Amuka-Bird (Luther) guest star

programme.desc: The clerical sleuth discovers that a murderer is using Kemblefords new model village to plan their attacks. Mark Williams stars, with Claudie Blakley and Aidan McArdle in this mystery drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton.

programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: The Mentalist
programme.desc: The body of a saleswoman is found in the boot of a car at a luxury showroom, and an autopsy reveals she was run over. As inquiries proceed, the agents begin to suspect her boyfriend is to blame, but Jane is unconvinced and tries to draw the real murderer out into the open - only to end up destroying a Lamborghini. Crime drama, starring Simon Baker

programme.title: Hudson and Rex
programme.desc: Romance is truly dead in St. Johns when a prominent member of an exclusive dating app is found slain with an arrow through his heart

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Cop-turned-private-eye Alexa investigates a case of death by stir fry when Reuben is desperate for her to clear the name of his friend, a private chef accused of poisoning one of his clients. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and the team are called upon to investigate the death of Fidels old school friend, Carlton Paris, who has been shot at home. The victim was last seen alive at an art gallery with Dorothy Foster, a wealthy older woman - and before long they discover he made his living as an escort and had left several disgruntled and heartbroken women in his wake, including a local judge and the wife of a club owner. But as the investigation progresses, Fidel struggles to keep his emotions in check, forcing him to confront some long-buried issues. Kris Marshall and Gary Carr star, with guest appearances from Adrian Scarborough, Sharon Small, Tristan Gemmill and Josette Simon

Five people are in the frame - the victims housekeeper, his PA and his three children - although when an approaching hurricane strands the cops on the island, knocking out the phones and computers, they are forced to rely on good old-fashioned investigative techniques. As evening approaches, the cops realise they will be spending the night in a house with a killer. Can they solve the mystery before he or she strikes again? Jimmy Akingbola (Holbys Antoine Malick) and Nikki Amuka-Bird (Luther) guest star

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: A mysterious saboteur tries to stop Kembleford winning the Three Counties choir competition. Return of the 1950s-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as the Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicias impromptu visit coincides with Father Brown helping out a friend at an exclusive gentlemens club. Mystery drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Humphrey and the team are called upon to investigate the death of Fidels old school friend, Carlton Paris, who has been shot at home. The victim was last seen alive at an art gallery with Dorothy Foster, a wealthy older woman - and before long they discover he made his living as an escort and had left several disgruntled and heartbroken women in his wake, including a local judge and the wife of a club owner. But as the investigation progresses, Fidel struggles to keep his emotions in check, forcing him to confront some long-buried issues. Kris Marshall and Gary Carr star, with guest appearances from Adrian Scarborough, Sharon Small, Tristan Gemmill and Josette Simon

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: Cop-turned-private-eye Alexa investigates a case of death by stir fry when Reuben is desperate for her to clear the name of his friend, a private chef accused of poisoning one of his clients. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When the criminal who murdered Detective Watts brother is released, Watts kills him. Discrepancies in his statement lead Murdoch to suspect that he took justice into his own hands

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch suspects murder at what first appears to be the double suicide of a wealthy high-society couple, who are found floating in their pool

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: When Brackenreid leaves Toronto to help an old friend find a missing woman, he learns hes a lot closer to the case than he thought

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: A young womans body is found in a seemingly idyllic Northumberland coastal village in 1966, and the subsequent investigation leads Gently and Bacchus to suspect the victims estranged husband is responsible for the killing. However, they soon come to realise her disturbed family is hiding an even more shocking secret. Starring Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby, with Daniel Casey (Midsomer Murders), Shaun Dooley (Married Single Other), Paul Kaye (Two Thousand Acres of Sky) and Greg Hemphill (Still Game)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself having to untangle the mysteries that lie within a sewing circle when a seamstress is stabbed to death. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Mrs Devine and Sullivans wedding approaches - and Father Brown finds himself having to prevent a would-be killer from ruining their big day. Fifties-set crime drama, starring Mark Williams, Claudie Blakley and Tom Chambers

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest finds himself embroiled with MI5, which puts Lady Felicia in a compromising position - will she betray her friend to protect secrets from her own past? Drama, starring Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The priest investigates when the new wife of Sir Raleigh is found dead in a locked room after seeing an Egyptian mummy. Crime drama, starring Mark Williams

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.desc: Comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon. Frank and Lu are drawn into Stratfords Silicon Valley when Spider calls upon their help to expose a mole within tech company Forosa. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star

programme.title: Shakespeare and Hathaway: Private Investigators
programme.sub-title: If Music Be the Food of Love
programme.desc: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of ballroom dancing, brimming with passion and jealousy - and learn the dangers of partners falling in love with one another. Guest starring Ruthie Henshall, Danny John-Jules and Leila Mimmack

programme.title: Miss Scarlet and the Duke
programme.desc: Dylan Cooper has been nabbed, but his gang plan to spring him out of the cells at Scotland Yard, leading to a long night for Blake and new detective George Willows

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: Murdoch investigates when a string of vicious animal attacks in High Park terrorise the residents of Toronto.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Lady Felicias lavish New Year ball is ruined when a VIP guest is murdered. Drama based on the stories of GK Chesterton, with Mark Williams and Nancy Carroll

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A birdwatcher is stabbed to death with his own knife, a case that holds a certain fascination for Humphrey, who was a keen twitcher in his youth. The victim was one of a group of enthusiasts who had visited the island for a glimpse of a famous parrot - but the team soon faces a conundrum as all the suspects were in full sight of one another when the crime was committed. As the cops search for answers, they uncover evidence of long-standing rivalries, secret jealousies and romantic entanglements. But would any of it justify killing a harmless ornithologist?

Kris Marshall and Danny John-Jules star in the light-hearted Caribbean crime drama

programme.desc: In this special festive edition of the award-winning show, Tom Allen plays host in front of a packed audience and welcomes Thanyia Moore and Lloyd Griffith to the stage

programme.desc: Actor and adventure enthusiast Orlando Bloom learns three extreme sports, wingsuiting, free diving and rock climbing, to test his limits of physical and mental endurance

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James charts the history of 4x4 vehicles from the jeeps of the Second World war onwards, revealing how they rose in popularity in everyday use and in rallies. He explores the rivalry between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, and puts some classic vehicles through their paces by driving up Mount Fuji and through the Mojave desert

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: US host Sean Kelly oversees UK bidders battling to win potential treasures found in storage containers, lock-ups and garages in Northumberland

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Rick Parfitt of Status Quo joins Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe as they show off the Peugeot RC concept car and enter a lively debate about transport and the environment. Plus, the team test-drives the Maserati 4200 GT, while vicars, rabbis and mullahs take to the track to prove which faith moves fastest

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe take a look at family cars. Jeremy shakes up the school run with a Volvo XC90 and the radical Renault Espace, Richard road-tests an interesting alternative to the usual estate, the Subaru Forester, and Jason presents a second-hand buyers guide to one of the cleverest MPVs around, the Vauxhall Zafira. Meanwhile, the storming Volkswagen Golf R32 becomes the latest power car to take on the Top Gear test track. Chef Gordon Ramsay is the star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the beautiful and unique landscapes of the Lake District, beginning by meeting Cumbrians with different visions for the future of Englands biggest national park. He talks to members of a campaign group trying to protect the remaining red squirrels, a traditional farmer who feels that his ancient way of life is on the point of extinction, and an 18-year-old student who runs a 1,500-acre farm

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: It is the start of the coarse fishing season and to celebrate Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse head to the Norfolk Broads to fish in some of East Anglias most historic and celebrated waterways. Theyre there to try for rudd - but who knows what other species they might catch in these rivers, which are rich in flora and fauna?

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse fish for wild carp in Mid Wales, spending their first day at an ancient glacial lake where Bob tries out new gear he is keen to use and hopes will make a difference to his fishing. During this trip, Bob and Paul meet Mick May and Marina Gibson, who together have started a charity called Cancer and Pisces, which offers fishing as therapeutic respite for people with cancer

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are on the River Severn in Worcestershire to fish for zander, and enlist the help of local guide Tim Maslen to find the best spots. As they sit and wait, they ruminate on the state of Britains rivers in general, the threats that they are under and what it takes to do something to help them improve. After a few hours they move down the river to a secluded beach and meet their special guest - local actor and self-confessed fishing addict Charlie Cooper, star of This Country

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate some of their favourite indulgent dishes for entertaining guests, receiving money-saving tips from Michelin-starred chef Tom Kitchin and delving into British culinary history for gourmet inspiration. Finally, they put everything they have learned to the test when they help a lifeboat helmsman say a surprise thank you to his crew

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate how to create a gourmet party on a tight budget, preparing cut-price canapes, cakes and tasty treats, with Michelin-starred chef Nigel Haworth giving advice on vegetarian food. Finally, they put everything they have learned to the test when they arrange a surprise celebration for 35 farm volunteers

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve

Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: City of London Police begin a dangerous high-speed chase when the UKs most notorious phone snatcher races into the Square Mile on an illegal electric bike

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britains most talented detectives pursue two gunmen killers in Northamptonshire, distraction burglars in sleepy Kent, and a multi-million-pound footwear scam in Manchester

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Matt Forde and Ayesha Hazarika joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the weeks events

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig asks questions on animals beginning with the letter R, with Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Alan Davies trying to come up with interesting answers - while avoiding the most obvious ones

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain returns with a timely new run of the topical comedy show, with Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, James Acaster, Milton Jones and Tez Ilyas joining regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look at whats been happening in the news through stand-up and improvised games

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians James Acaster, Ed Gamble, Rhys James, Nish Kumar and Zoe Lyons. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and regular panellist Hugh Dennis look back on another eventful week in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Angela Barnes, Gary Delaney, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman and Nish Kumar. Stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Radio 1 DJ Clara Amfo, actor Tom Davis, actor and musician Shaun Ryder and comedian Henning Wehn, aiming to deceive their opponents with plausible lies. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition. Sue Perkins, Reginald D Hunter and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies on the comedy quiz. Host Stephen Fry asks a range of fiendish questions on the topic of kitsch, with points being awarded for interesting answers as well as correct ones

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.desc: In the show finale, David gets stressed about the weather on his wedding day. Thankfully, his family are on hand to make everything right

programme.title: Schitts Creek
programme.sub-title: Best Wishes, Warmest Regards: A Schitts Creek Farewell
programme.desc: An inside look at the final season of the comedy series starring Eugene Levy including unseen footage.

programme.title: Gadget Man
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade explores the exhausting world of parenting in this edition, testing a self-assembling pram and a £10,000 robotic babysitter with the help of Denise Van Outen. He also looks at a selection of gadgets for older children with Hugh Dennis, from a mind-controlled helicopter to the latest in interactive computer gaming

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: James Mays Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the worlds fastest driverless car

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A woman is suspected of smuggling someone elses baby over the border. Drugs are found hidden in a consignment of carrots. A pill smuggler is caught following an earlier tip-off

programme.title: Border Force: Americas Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A man travelling with two children that arent his is questioned, a traveller is caught smuggling cash, and officers find drugs in a flat-bed truck

programme.title: Aussie Road Train Truckers
programme.desc: Brownie is not at all pleased when he is covered in slop trying to load up hundreds of dirty cattle onto his road train, and Jake hits a deep crack in a dirt road

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Cornwall has more than its fair share of folktales. Perhaps the most famous is found near Lands End, where Rick recounts the tale of the Mermaid of Zennor. A short distance away, he joins a small army of seaweed harvesters who are feeding an increasing appetite for a natural food more common in Japan

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: Footballer Raheem Sterling is one of a string of multi-millionaires targeted by a highly-organised burglary gang being pursued by Surrey police

programme.title: Special Ops: To Catch a Criminal
programme.desc: When a woman reports being held against her will, Thames Valley Police suspect modern-day slavery and are shocked by the identity of the main suspect

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.sub-title: Speed with Guy Martin: Classic F1 Special
programme.desc: Guy takes part in his first-ever Formula 1 race as he goes head to head with 2009 world champion Jenson Button around Silverstone Grand Prix circuit. Guy is driving a classic 1983 Williams FW08c, working for six months with the Williams heritage Division to restore it from museum piece to racing condition, and learned how to drive Formula 1 style under the direction of Karun Chandhok, formerly of the Hispania Racing F1 Team, while Jenson takes to the track in a prototype six-wheeled Williams from the 1980s

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris take sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip. Back on the track, Chris tests out Volkswagens ID-R electric racer. YouTube star KSI is the studio guest

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris put emergency vehicles to the test to see if the fire brigade, the police or the ambulance service has the fastest vehicles. Chris pays tribute to rally driver Colin McRae, Paddy puts the new BMW M8 through its paces, and comedian Tom Allen joins the team in the studio

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers: Mums Know Best
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers examine a variety of traditional dishes reserved for birthdays, learning how to make Manchester tart, Greek stuffed vine leaves and a Caribbean Saturday soup. They also demonstrate how to prepare a summer berry trifle, and invite 200 women to sample one anothers specialities

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Cornwall has more than its fair share of folktales. Perhaps the most famous is found near Lands End, where Rick recounts the tale of the Mermaid of Zennor. A short distance away, he joins a small army of seaweed harvesters who are feeding an increasing appetite for a natural food more common in Japan

programme.title: Rick Steins Cornwall
programme.desc: Rick roams the dramatic north Cornish coast to tell the story of one of Britains best-loved writers, Thomas Hardy, and his little-known love affair with a Cornishwoman. Another famous writer, Dylan Thomas, once dubbed Ricks next stop, Mousehole, as the loveliest village in Britain. Its here that Rick discovers the origins of the iconic Cornish dish stargazy pie

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian Bob Mortimer and actress Patsy Kensit join team captain Lee Mack, while comedy actor Greg Davies and Pointless co-host Richard Osman are enlisted by his counterpart David Mitchell. Rob Brydon oversees proceedings as the two sides engage in a battle of wits to determine which of them can most successfully stretch the truth about their own lives

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Dermot OLeary, Mel Giedroyc, Josh Widdicombe and Matt Dawson join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack for the comedy panel show, trying to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Rob Brydon hosts

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe take a look at family cars. Jeremy shakes up the school run with a Volvo XC90 and the radical Renault Espace, Richard road-tests an interesting alternative to the usual estate, the Subaru Forester, and Jason presents a second-hand buyers guide to one of the cleverest MPVs around, the Vauxhall Zafira. Meanwhile, the storming Volkswagen Golf R32 becomes the latest power car to take on the Top Gear test track. Chef Gordon Ramsay is the star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Caribbean with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins the last leg of his journey in Nicaragua and visits the Rama-Kriol people, who face losing their ancestral homes due to the construction of a new transoceanic waterway. Simon then discovers Hondurans living in the grip of some of the most violent criminal gangs in the world, with San Pedro Sula having the highest murder rate of any city on the planet. His journey ends in Jamaica, where he spends time with young people who have rejected gang life, offering a model of hope for future generations

programme.title: The Lakes with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The adventurer explores the beautiful and unique landscapes of the Lake District, beginning by meeting Cumbrians with different visions for the future of Englands biggest national park. He talks to members of a campaign group trying to protect the remaining red squirrels, a traditional farmer who feels that his ancient way of life is on the point of extinction, and an 18-year-old student who runs a 1,500-acre farm

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: It is the start of the coarse fishing season and to celebrate Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse head to the Norfolk Broads to fish in some of East Anglias most historic and celebrated waterways. Theyre there to try for rudd - but who knows what other species they might catch in these rivers, which are rich in flora and fauna?

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer and Paul Whitehouse fish for wild carp in Mid Wales, spending their first day at an ancient glacial lake where Bob tries out new gear he is keen to use and hopes will make a difference to his fishing. During this trip, Bob and Paul meet Mick May and Marina Gibson, who together have started a charity called Cancer and Pisces, which offers fishing as therapeutic respite for people with cancer

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are on the River Severn in Worcestershire to fish for zander, and enlist the help of local guide Tim Maslen to find the best spots. As they sit and wait, they ruminate on the state of Britains rivers in general, the threats that they are under and what it takes to do something to help them improve. After a few hours they move down the river to a secluded beach and meet their special guest - local actor and self-confessed fishing addict Charlie Cooper, star of This Country

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Everyday Gourmets
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers demonstrate some of their favourite indulgent dishes for entertaining guests, receiving money-saving tips from Michelin-starred chef Tom Kitchin and delving into British culinary history for gourmet inspiration. programme.desc: The nurses and midwives of Nonnatus House prepare to celebrate Sister Monica Joans birthday, but plans are put on hold when the closure of a nearby maternity home leaves them inundated with new patients, many of whom are in urgent need of support. Lucille treats a woman whose father is offering shelter to an old friend suffering with memory loss, and as November 22, 1963 arrives, the residents of Poplar are rocked by shocking news from America

Lloyd Owen stars, with Catherine Zeta-Jones, Tom Bell and Maria Mile

Starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team reinvestigates the death of wealthy financier Douglas Anderson when psychic Sebastian Carter informs the deceaseds daughter that she needs to resolve her fathers unfinished business. The retired officers set out to prove the clairvoyant is a fraud after growing suspicious of his gift, but Pullman struggles to dismiss the supposed conman when a conflict involving her own late father emerges. Detective drama, guest starring David Bradley

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe
programme.desc: The duo are called in to find the murderer of an eight-year-old girl, a case which dredges up disturbing memories for Dalziel, who investigated a similar incident in the same area 15 years earlier. The townsfolk are sure a missing local man is guilty of both crimes, but new evidence soon points toward a more complex solution. Feature-length detective drama, starring Warren Clarke, Colin Buchanan, Joanna David, Ronald Pickup and Kaye Wragg

programme.title: Whitstable Pearl
programme.sub-title: The Gumshoe and the Femme Fatale
programme.desc: After changing her will to leave everything to her housekeeper, an aging actress is attacked. Mike and Pearl clash over who they believe is responsible

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James treats a dog with a confusing complaint, Siegfried tries a herbal remedy on a sick sheep, and Tristan heads off to London for the Coronation. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.sub-title: Wipe That Smile off Your Tape
programme.desc: Sharon joins a video dating agency and asks Darren to shoot a rehearsal tape, while Garth buys tickets for a Rolling Stones concert

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: The focus turns to birds, revealing the role of their feathers, the ways in which they attract partners and the challenges they face during the nesting season. programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Timothy West and Prunella Scales embark on a journey into their pasts via the Leeds and Liverpool Canal, which celebrated its 200th anniversary in 2016. Along the way, they visit the countrys last surviving steam-powered textile mill, meander through the curlies, and meet acclaimed poet Simon Armitage. They also take a trip by steam train to the home of the Bronte sisters in Haworth, and are confronted by the Salts Mill after tackling the steepest lock staircase in Britain

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which Timothy West and Prunella Scales explore India by canal boat. The trip begins in Kerala, a region of palm-fringed lakes, rivers and British-built canals which has had a thriving spice trade for thousands of years. They explore a boat built by a local ruler and make offerings at a temple, while Pru learns a traditional dance

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how mammals dominate the planet and survive in extreme habitats. In the Antarctic, a lone Weddell seal wears its teeth down keeping an ice hole open to catch fish for its pup. In warmer climes, the gerbil-like rufous sengi demonstrates impressive speed to avoid reptilian predators, giant bats feast on fruit trees in Zambia and male humpback whales battle for a female

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to fish, the most diverse vertebrates on the planet, made up of more than 28,000 species. Off the coast of South Australia, sea dragons pair up for courtship, engaging in a mirror dance before the eggs are laid on the males tail. Gobies climb up the vertical rocks of Hawaiian waterfalls to access secluded pools at the summit, and flying fish use their elongated pectoral fins to escape from predators. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to birds, revealing the role of their feathers, the ways in which they attract partners and the challenges they face during the nesting season. Narrator David Attenborough provides an insight into the courtship spectacle of the lesser flamingo on Kenyas Lake Bogoria, and follows chinstrap penguins in Antarctica, which make an exhausting climb up a volcano to feed their young

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to insects, whose key to success is an armoured covering that takes on almost any shape. Bombardier beetles fire boiling liquid at enemies through a rotating nozzle, while a honey bee army stings a raiding bear into submission. Grass-cutter ants march like a Roman army and cultivate a fungus to break down the grass they have harvested. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys

En route, Michael travels through the huge Northumberland coalfield and, at Cramlington, he alights to visit an extraordinary work of landform art made from the spoil of a coalmine, the vast Northumberlandia, known as the Lady of the North

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos Scottish coastal railway journey reaches Invergordon on the Cromarty Firth, the third great inlet on the northeast coast of the Highlands. He arrives on Britains northernmost railway, the Far North Line, and heads straight onto the water to explore a former oil drilling rig now moored in the firth

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length of the Northumbrian coastline, with its glorious beaches and grandiose castles. In the village of Alnmouth he takes to the waves in a traditional skiff built by members of the Alnmouth Coastal Rowing Club. His last stop of the day is the mighty Bamburgh Castle

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Documentary following the exploits of a family-run crew of heavy-haulage specialists as they transport the heaviest, longest and most precious locomotives around the world. Terry and Jason have been called in to move legendary steam locomotive Britannia. The 68-year-old vehicle has undergone a major restoration in Crewe and is scheduled to start running at the Severn Valley Railway

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move an iconic Deltic diesel, better known as The Royal Highland Fusilier, halfway across the country to the Telford Steam Fair

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Zak and Alex face a four-day mission to deliver a pair of classic steam locomotives for an Autumn Steam Gala at the Mid Hants Railway in Hampshire

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Heavy haulage experts Ian Bagladi and Jamie Turnbull need to move a classical but abandoned 63-year-old, 70-tonne locomotive, through the English countryside

programme.title: Englands Forgotten Queen: The Life and Death of Lady Jane Grey
programme.desc: Helen Castor concludes the docudrama telling the story of the woman who reigned England and Ireland for nine days in 1553. The historian looks at the final days of her reign, when she had been installed in the Tower of London, only for Mary Tudors forces to assemble ready for battle. And when one of Janes keys supporters defected to Marys side, taking thousands of supporters, the balance tipped and the men who installed her on the throne abandoned her

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets off from Tynemouth on the first leg of a trip to Berwick-upon-Tweed, hearing about of a 19th-century tragedy at sea that inspired 140 local men to set up a coastal rescue service. At Long Sands beach, Michael rolls up his sleeves to help volunteers caring for sick and injured seals at the Tynemouth Aquarium Seal Hospital

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo visits Leuchars, known nowadays on the railways as Leuchars for St Andrews, which is Scotlands oldest university and is located on a beautiful beachfront

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his exploration of Scotlands spectacular west coast from the Firth of Clyde to the Outer Hebrides. From the 250-year old new town of Helensburgh on the north bank of the Clyde, Michael takes his favourite West Highland Line across epic landscapes to Garelochhead and Ben Cruachan, and makes his way to Connel Bridge and the waters of Loch Etive

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville buys a batch of devices that are supposed to create positive emotions. A local underworld boss is looking for Leroy after his latest girlfriend makes Mrs Hussein jealous, while Mrs Featherstone takes it upon herself to solve Mr Newbolds delicate problem - with the long-suffering shopkeepers help

programme.title: Im Alan Partridge
programme.sub-title: I Know What Alan Did Last Summer
programme.desc: Alan fears a visit from the Inland Revenue may be imminent, while Sonja discovers he has lied about his close ties with rock star Bono. Steve Coogan and Amelia Bullmore star

programme.title: Im Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Poor sales see Alans book, Bouncing Back, scheduled to be pulped. However, he is somewhat consoled by his new house being finally completed. After offending a guest on a religious radio programme, he manages to upset other religiously minded guests at Lynns baptism ceremony. Steve Coogan stars

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: East End villains Harry and Annie Blackwood were rotten to the core and did some truly horrible things when they were alive. Now, their sons want to discover the truth about them. What secrets did they take to their grave? Comedy drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Anita Dobson and Phil Daniels

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Gareth is definitely going to be working from home today, in case anything unlucky happens, because it just happens to be Friday the 13th. Comedy drama written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Amanda Abbington and Samantha Spiro

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: You get to meet all sorts when you go online dating, as Vickys about to find out. Will she finally meet her perfect match, or will Vicky be talking to the Lonely Hearts Killer? Dark comedy written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Claire Rushbrook, Mathew Horne, Frances Barber and Asim Chaudhry

programme.title: Toast of London
programme.desc: Return of the comedy about a classically trained actor living in Soho, starring Matt Berry. Steven is intrigued to learn Ed is entering a celebrities and prostitutes blow-football tournament and soon becomes obsessed with taking part and winning the competition himself. When his first choice, Mrs Purchase, declines his invitation to partner him, Toast turns to legendary escort Wendy Nook. With Robert Bathurst, Tracy-Ann Oberman and Louise Jameson

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del falls for a glamorous antiques dealer who comes to view an old cabinet described as a Queen Anne original - but the feeling is not necessarily mutual. Comedy, starring David Jason and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Rodney tries to help a drunken woman on the street, but his good deed backfires. Fearful of a life behind bars, he promptly goes into hiding - unaware devious brother Del has set him up as a joke. Comedy, starring Nicholas Lyndhurst and David Jason

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters embark on a venture involving the decoration of Denzils flat. When they find his wifes beloved canary lying dead in its cage, they assume responsibility and race to replace it before the deal is ruined. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodneys Christmas celebrations are disrupted by the unexpected arrival of their estranged father - leaving the brothers doubtful they can trust him after such a long absence. Another chance to see the 1983 festive special of John Sullivans comedy

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del buys a new manual to help him show Raquel more appreciation and give him the confidence to make positive decisions. Unfortunately, as a result of such upbeat thinking, the Trotters cash flow hits an all-time low and an angry punter is left baying for blood. The second part of the Christmas 1996 trilogy, starring David Jason, Tessa Peake-Jones and Nicholas Lyndhurst

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: The men head off for a weekend at a training camp, but arrive late and hungry after Mainwarings map-reading skills are found to be somewhat lacking. They then have to capture an enemys camp during an exercise, giving the captain a chance to earn back his mens respect. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville is determined to play Cupid for Madge and Gastric, but he realises he will stand no chance of matchmaking the pair unless the latter improves his image. His plans take an inspired turn, however, when a salesman returns to town. Wet Eric is desperate to convince his wife its going to rain and gets lured into a dangerous purchase, and Granville sets his sights on Old Bentinck, who never goes into the shop, coming up with a cunning way to tempt him in to spend. David Jason, Mark Williams, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville buys a batch of devices that are supposed to create positive emotions. A local underworld boss is looking for Leroy after his latest girlfriend makes Mrs Hussein jealous, while Mrs Featherstone takes it upon herself to solve Mr Newbolds delicate problem - with the long-suffering shopkeepers help

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: A special programme focusing on the delicate issue of the MeToo movement gives Alan a chance to show off his broadcasting skills. Plus, there is a live performance by Alans very own mixed vocal harmony group the Quavers. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, with Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Faced with some of the toughest challenges that live TV can throw at a man - including rancour behind the scenes, a viewer in danger and a segment on womens fashion - Alan calls on Sidekick Simon Denton to step up to the plate. But will they sink or swim? Steve Coogan stars as the broadcaster making a return to prime-time TV from the wilderness of local radio when he is given the opportunity of co-hosting BBC One magazine show This Time. With Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu and Tim Key

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville is determined to play Cupid for Madge and Gastric, but he realises he will stand no chance of matchmaking the pair unless the latter improves his image. David Jason, Mark Williams, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville has to deal with a malfunctioning till, while Eric and Cyril decide it is time to show some macho spirit in the home of the Tour de Yorkshire and get back in the saddle. Meanwhile, Leroy tells Beth about his sneaky plan to find a blind date for Ruby, and Mr Newbold is sent on a mission by Mrs Featherstone to find out more about Mrs Rossi and whether she poses a threat to the local shop. But does he discover more than he bargained for?

programme.title: Mrs Browns Boys
programme.desc: It seems that everyone in the Brown family has a date for Valentines Day except Agnes, so she turns to the internet to find herself a man. Dermot tries to come up with a loving gesture to prove to Maria he can still be romantic - but he makes the mistake of taking advice from Buster - while Agnes and Winnie are fascinated by Bettys sex manual and Grandad ends up addicted to Viagra. Hit comedy, starring Brendan OCarroll

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Deborah and Dorothy go away for a sailing weekend, leaving the men to get up to no end of mischief as Tony converts the shed into a sauna and Gary gets to grips with a nubile neighbour. Starring Martin Clunes, Neil Morrissey and Liz Carling

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the comedy sketch show. Tim Nice-But-Dim takes over an antiques shop, the lovely wobbly randy old ladies go to the launderette, Kevin reaches a landmark birthday and Ben Elton becomes a feminist version of Benny Hill. Plus, Waynetta kicks Wayne out. With Paul Whitehouse and Kathy Burke

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Three pairs compete for a big cash prize in a new general knowledge quiz hosted by Lee Mack. But the tension proves too much for one of the contestants. Black comedy, written by Reece Shearsmith and Steve Pemberton

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Joe and Chas have been together for nine years, a long time to be truly in love with someone. But how long does it take to properly say goodbye? Darkly comic tale, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with a guest appearance from Sheila Reid

programme.title: Toast of London
programme.desc: Steven receives a huge bill after Ray Purchase reports him for unpaid taxes, forcing the actor to take on some particularly unattractive jobs to pay off the debt. Comedy, starring Matt Berry, featuring a guest appearance by John Nettles

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: Comedy about a self-made man struggling to keep his head above water as the 1980s turn into the 1990s. Martin Parker has the ambition of Alan Sugar and the swagger of Robert Kilroy Silk. With a chain of electrical stores bearing his name, he seems to have it all. However the truth is he swimming in debt, his complicated private life is catching up with him and hes starting to lose his power. Written by and starring Sian Gibson, with Conleth Hill, Rosie Cavaliero, George Costigan, Sheila Reid and Jason Barnett

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: The morning after the night before has not started well. Diane is worried that she did not impress the school governors, Kath is revealing more than she should to the women in the old peoples home and Martin is desperately trying to get his hands on a chunk of cash

programme.title: Toast of London
programme.desc: Stevens return to the West End stage in legendary whodunit The Moose Trap is seriously jeopardised when he reveals the killers identity in a radio interview. As attendance numbers at the theatre dwindle, the actor is forced to look elsewhere for employment, and when the job of the announcer on a university quiz show becomes available, hes very keen to land the role. programme.desc: Rodney tries to help a drunken woman on the street, but his good deed backfires. Fearful of a life behind bars, he promptly goes into hiding - unaware devious brother Del has set him up as a joke. Comedy, starring Nicholas Lyndhurst and David Jason

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: The Trotters embark on a venture involving the decoration of Denzils flat. When they find his wifes beloved canary lying dead in its cage, they assume responsibility and race to replace it before the deal is ruined. Comedy, with David Jason, Nicholas Lyndhurst and Lennard Pearce

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses
programme.desc: Del and Rodneys Christmas celebrations are disrupted by the unexpected arrival of their estranged father - leaving the brothers doubtful they can trust him after such a long absence. Another chance to see the 1983 festive special of John Sullivans comedy

programme.title: Dads Army
programme.desc: The men head off for a weekend at a training camp, but arrive late and hungry after Mainwarings map-reading skills are found to be somewhat lacking. They then have to capture an enemys camp during an exercise, giving the captain a chance to earn back his mens respect. Comedy first broadcast in 1969, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville is determined to play Cupid for Madge and Gastric, but he realises he will stand no chance of matchmaking the pair unless the latter improves his image. His plans take an inspired turn, however, when a salesman returns to town. Wet Eric is desperate to convince his wife its going to rain and gets lured into a dangerous purchase, and Granville sets his sights on Old Bentinck, who never goes into the shop, coming up with a cunning way to tempt him in to spend. David Jason, Mark Williams, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville buys a batch of devices that are supposed to create positive emotions. programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Faced with some of the toughest challenges that live TV can throw at a man - including rancour behind the scenes, a viewer in danger and a segment on womens fashion - Alan calls on Sidekick Simon Denton to step up to the plate. But will they sink or swim? Steve Coogan stars as the broadcaster making a return to prime-time TV from the wilderness of local radio when he is given the opportunity of co-hosting BBC One magazine show This Time. programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: You get to meet all sorts when you go online dating, as Vickys about to find out. Will she finally meet her perfect match, or will Vicky be talking to the Lonely Hearts Killer? Dark comedy written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Claire Rushbrook, Mathew Horne, Frances Barber and Asim Chaudhry

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Three pairs compete for a big cash prize in a new general knowledge quiz hosted by Lee Mack. But the tension proves too much for one of the contestants. Black comedy, written by Reece Shearsmith and Steve Pemberton

programme.title: Inside No 9

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: Comedy about a self-made man struggling to keep his head above water as the 1980s turn into the 1990s. Martin Parker has the ambition of Alan Sugar and the swagger of Robert Kilroy Silk. With a chain of electrical stores bearing his name, he seems to have it all. However the truth is he swimming in debt, his complicated private life is catching up with him and hes starting to lose his power. Written by and starring Sian Gibson, with Conleth Hill, Rosie Cavaliero, George Costigan, Sheila Reid and Jason Barnett

programme.title: The Power of Parker
programme.desc: The morning after the night before has not started well. Diane is worried that she did not impress the school governors, Kath is revealing more than she should to the women in the old peoples home and Martin is desperately trying to get his hands on a chunk of cash

programme.title: Toast of London
programme.desc: Stevens return to the West End stage in legendary whodunit The Moose Trap is seriously jeopardised when he reveals the killers identity in a radio interview. As attendance numbers at the theatre dwindle, the actor is forced to look elsewhere for employment, and when the job of the announcer on a university quiz show becomes available, hes very keen to land the role. But first he will have to impress the fearsome - and famously rude - host

programme.desc: Stacey coaches the Fall family through getting rid of four years worth of clutter including 2,137 toys, 452 books, 13 guitars, 61 mugs and 1,274 tools. Emotions run high when Dilly presents the family with £2,000 in cash and cheques found hidden in the mess of their overflowing home

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis interviews some of the biggest names in UK entertainment and is granted exclusive access into their lives at home and at work, beginning with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south-west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life; from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models

programme.sub-title: Hes Just Not That into Ben

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben discovers he is quick to make new friends following a robbery and a case of mistaken identity. Guest starring Phil Davis (Whitechapel)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: In a move that could spell disaster, Ben volunteers to look after one of Janeys elderly clients for the evening. Comedy, guest starring Keith Barron

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers go their separate ways after a big argument. While Ben moves in with Roger - much to the dismay of his friend, who is expecting a date - Susan throws a party to celebrate her new-found freedom. However, anything she can do, he can do better, and the dentist sets about faking his own party to make his wife jealous. Zoe Wanamaker and Robert Lindsay star as the warring couple, with Doreen Mantle (One Foot in the Grave)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Kenzos father asks for Janeys hand in marriage, but she is already smitten with good-looking Australian Craig - and Susan is thrilled to learn he is also planning a proposal

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben is outraged by the introduction of timecards at work, so he takes a stand by forming a union - but his boss Mr Griffith responds by making him a tempting offer. Susan prepares for her audition to become a childrens TV presenter, but is surprised to discover her main rival is Janey

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The pioneering vet treats a 10-year-old beagle unable to stand on its back legs, and when a scan reveals the canine has two diseased discs between three of its neck vertebrae, he operates to fuse the bones. He also fits a bionic knee in an 11-year-old labrador with bone cancer in its leg, and treats a bulldog puppy that has been run over by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old dachshund is rushed to Fitzpatricks by a distraught friend of the owner after being run over by a driver and sustaining life-threatening injuries. A couple arrive with their nine-year-old schnauzer, which is booked in for complex spinal surgery after an earlier operation on its knee, and the owners of a shih tzu bring in their pet, which has been injured in an accident at home

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition following Noel Fitzpatrick as he treats former rescue dogs who have found a new life as family pets, but are still plagued by medical problems. The patients include a shih tzu with damaged discs that are treated with special supports, small pins and cement, a bulldog puppy with a fracture that extends across a growth plate, and a German shepherd that has had a leg amputated, and has developed a hip problem from walking on three legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Beloved fluff monster Kylo, a 10-month-old rough collie, and golden retriever pup Sasha make return visits to the practice. Kylos serious leg deformities are causing significant pain, while Sashas deformed front left foot has left her without properly developed toes - and her struggles are increasing the more she grows. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team need to find innovative solutions to resolve the problems both hounds present

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolos leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noels own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews struggle to reach a woman who has fallen from her horse on treacherous boggy ground. Paramedics also treat a 14-year-old boy in agony with suspected appendicitis

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics attend a father and son whose car has been hit by a falling tree on a dark and stormy night, and are called out to assist a homeless woman sheltering in a bin shed

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A four-year-old girl has her droopy eyelid lifted, a rugby player gets his broken nose reset and a woman has an operation on her wrist after almost a year of agony

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: Nick has an ultrasound taken from inside his body to find out if he needs open-heart surgery, and Irene has gel removed from her eye to make her see again

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren gets a tattoo of her boyfriend, only to be immediately dumped, and Nan is introduced to her grandsons girlfriend. New bride Victoria makes a memorable wedding speech in which she discloses the number of relationships she has had in the 15 years since she became sexually active

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse appears as Sams dad in the comedy sketch show, while Elaine Figgis turns to the internet to find her perfect partner

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A stay-at-home mum who runs a picture-perfect household with her detail-obsessed husband moves in with a laid-back, hard-partying family with no rules

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A circus wife with an overworked sword-swallowing husband trades places with an overwhelmed mother of four rambunctious boys with an antique-addicted spouse

programme.title: Death at the Mansion: Rebecca Zahau
programme.desc: The investigation into the mysterious death of Rebecca Zahau, whose body was found at her billionaire boyfriends mansion, just two days after his six-year-old son died while in her care. The court ruled her death a suicide - but what really happened?

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out with Stacey Solomon
programme.desc: Stacey helps the Viney family declutter their home after grandma Sue filled their house with her deceased mums belongings and the contents of a recently closed gift shop. Sues children are desperate for a clear out so they can come over and spend more time with her. The team remove everything from the house including 532 plastic carrier bags, 205 hair rollers, 30 umbrellas, 17 digital cameras and 443 pairs of shoes. While the family sort through what they want to keep, recycle, donate or sell, carpenter Rob installs a pulldown bed for extra space and Dilly reinstates the downstairs as a functioning living and dining space

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews
programme.desc: Louis meets the Oscar-winning actress at her home in Surrey for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, her life away from acting and her relationship with her husband, the late Michael Williams. Louis also accompanies Judi to the Gielgud Theatre in Londons West End, where they discuss the difficulties of the lockdown and how it inadvertently led to her surprise popularity on TikTok

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The pioneering vet treats a 10-year-old beagle unable to stand on its back legs, and when a scan reveals the canine has two diseased discs between three of its neck vertebrae, he operates to fuse the bones. He also fits a bionic knee in an 11-year-old labrador with bone cancer in its leg, and treats a bulldog puppy that has been run over by a car

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A two-year-old dachshund is rushed to Fitzpatricks by a distraught friend of the owner after being run over by a driver and sustaining life-threatening injuries. A couple arrive with their nine-year-old schnauzer, which is booked in for complex spinal surgery after an earlier operation on its knee, and the owners of a shih tzu bring in their pet, which has been injured in an accident at home

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Festive edition following Noel Fitzpatrick as he treats former rescue dogs who have found a new life as family pets, but are still plagued by medical problems. The patients include a shih tzu with damaged discs that are treated with special supports, small pins and cement, a bulldog puppy with a fracture that extends across a growth plate, and a German shepherd that has had a leg amputated, and has developed a hip problem from walking on three legs

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Beloved fluff monster Kylo, a 10-month-old rough collie, and golden retriever pup Sasha make return visits to the practice. Kylos serious leg deformities are causing significant pain, while Sashas deformed front left foot has left her without properly developed toes - and her struggles are increasing the more she grows. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team need to find innovative solutions to resolve the problems both hounds present

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A 10-year-old Dobermann is treated for an aggressive bone tumour in its leg. One option is a full limb amputation, but another could potentially save Rolos leg by cutting out the affected part of the bone and replacing it with a unique implant of Noels own invention - a scapula-humeral endoprosthesis. Five-year-old labrador Jeff is suffering from developmental elbow disease, which cannot be managed by medication alone. The day of his planned surgery for a full elbow replacement arrives, though bad news places the prospect of such treatment in jeopardy

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews struggle to reach a woman who has fallen from her horse on treacherous boggy ground. Paramedics also treat a 14-year-old boy in agony with suspected appendicitis

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A four-year-old girl has her droopy eyelid lifted, a rugby player gets his broken nose reset and a woman has an operation on her wrist after almost a year of agony

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show

The team remove everything from the house including 532 plastic carrier bags, 205 hair rollers, 30 umbrellas, 17 digital cameras and 443 pairs of shoes. programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: Mrs Chambers takes it on herself to ensure her daughters wedding is fit for a queen, but her over-the-top ideas soon meet with disapproval. Stirred up by Terrys ridicule, the men rebel

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Up for auction this week is a 1980 Ford Escort, a hand-built 1946 Bedford 12 electric car, and a beautiful, red 1957 Austin Healey.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek collects a beautifully restored and carefully modified 1936 Wolseley 14/56. Meanwhile, a Batman-inspired Chrysler Crossfire is dropped off at the Thornton le Dale garage

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Pauls doing the pick-ups in Lancashire where a rare 1960 Vauxhall Cresta PA Friary estate in dusky pink awaits

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Bohemia and Bavaria, beginning by exploring the art nouveau architecture of Czech Republic capital Prague. He then samples the sulphurous spa waters and wallows in peat and mud in Marianske Lazne, formerly known as Marienbad, before test driving a state-of-the-art passenger train made at the Skoda factory in Pilsen. Crossing the border into Germany, Michael encounters a fire-breathing dragon in Furth-im-Wald, and learns about an early 20th-century pioneer who laid the foundations for Munichs pre-eminence in science and technology

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sets out to sample the delights of the Atlantic coasts of Spain and France, beginning in Bordeaux, where he uncovers a historic British connection to the regions wines. Across the border in San Sebastian, Michael learns how diplomacy brought Britain and Spain closer together and rides on a hair-raising scenic railway. Heading to Bilbao, he explores the industrial ties between France and Spain and learns to cook a traditional Basque dish

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The longest retreat in British military history, when Allied troops defending Burma in 1942 were confronted by Japanese forces well prepared for jungle warfare. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: The Luftwaffes bombing raids on Britains provincial cities, when Coventry and Plymouth were hardest hit, although Portsmouth, Glasgow, Sheffield and Bristol also suffered heavy casualties. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: How the morale of the Third Reich, boosted by Germanys initial military successes, was shattered by the devastating defeat at Stalingrad, relentless Allied bombing raids and an attempt on Hitlers life. Narrated by Laurence Olivier

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates the story of the D-Day landings in June 1944, when the Allies launched the biggest ever amphibious invasion on the beaches of Normandy. Although both sides suffered heavy casualties, it proved to be the turning point in the war against Germany

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across the North and the Midlands, Robbie faces issues with ice on the Grand Union Canal. He takes on Foxtons famous lock flight in Leicestershire and completes his journey in the picturesque village Braunston, Northamptonshire, regarded as the spiritual home for narrowboaters

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming explores life on the English canal network. A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves to be problematic and Robbie helps out a stranded boater

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming journeys through Royal Leamington Spa and the historic town of Warwick with its medieval castle.

programme.title: Britains Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet dives below the waves in search of the inspiration for the first stone circle, while Chris Packham and Neil Oliver spend the night on an abandoned island as they hunt for clues as to why cultures change. Meanwhile, Shini Somara tests the technology behind a bronze age sauna and the archaeologists uncover a remarkable find

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through some of the most remote landscapes on Earth. He begins in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, encountering thick jungles and tropical swamps as he looks for the people and wildlife whose home is Africas greatest rainforest. His first destination is the remote village of Baka, where Simon experiences a culture based on sharing resources and respecting all living things in the rainforest

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels through Patagonia at the foot of the South American continent, a beautiful and rugged land of mountains, ice and endless wild grasslands, His journey takes him up to the huge South Patagonian ice field, through the foothills of the Andes towards the great Grey Glacier of eastern Chile. In Taibo and Jauny, Simon encounters two gauchos, the cowboys of South America, who live isolated lives in the mountains, tending their livestock and braving the storms in a simple hut for months on end

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek acquires a collection of pre and post war classics, including a Vincent Rapide, a 1928 Alvis, a Ford Pilot and an Indian Scout with sidecar

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New Yorks skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.sub-title: To the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earths poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Its Robbie Cummings toughest trip yet as he battles through water weed on the Bradley Arm and gets stuck in silt on the Ridgacre Branch

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: In Luton Airport No One Can Hear You Scream
programme.desc: The Meldrews return from an overseas holiday to find yet another disaster awaiting them - their home has been demolished. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.sub-title: We Have Put Her Living in the Tomb
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret agree to pet-sit a tortoise for a friend who ought to know better, and an anniversary outing to the theatre ends in disaster. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household echoes to the sound of laughter, as Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and Victor begins penning a sitcom script. However, his mother-in-laws bath-time accident and a run-in with British Telecom soon leave the curmudgeonly codger in no mood for jokes. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the lives of plants in environments around the world, beginning with the plant battlegrounds of the tropical rainforests. New filming techniques allow viewers to enter the plants world and see it from their perspective, and on their timescale. Featuring fast-growing trees, flowers that mimic dead animals, and life and death battles to absorb much-needed sunlight

programme.title: Whatever Happened to the Likely Lads?
programme.desc: On the eve of his wedding, Bob turns down the chance of going on a drunken rampage in favour of a quiet night in. Classic comedy, starring Rodney Bewes and James Bolam

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A low-mileage Renault Dauphine proves irresistible to the team, but its 1959 mechanics ensure some hefty bills

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team, including Andy Joynson, buy the right kind of Land Rover, but its got the wrong engine. Can they clean up its emissions with a modern motor?

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures once more on to the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaws 1913 publication Continental Guide, beginning by travelling through Russia. He starts in the industrial city of Tula, before visiting Leo Tolstoys former home of Yasnaya Polyana and learning how the authors life and works were intertwined with the railways. Michael then boards the train that runs from the Caspian Sea to Moscow, where he performs an important role in a dramatic opera at the Bolshoi Theatre, before exploring the beauty and history of St Petersburg and riding on the first railway ever built in Russia

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1913 Bradshaw railway guide, Michael Portillo takes the train down the spine of Italy as he travels from Rome to Sicily. He begins by weaving among the capital citys landmarks on the back of a 1950s Vespa, before boarding the train south to Naples, where he finds out about the first railway to be built in the country, and ventures into the crater of Mount Vesuvius. He takes a detour to the island of Capri, before finishing his journey in the ancient hilltop town of Taormina

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team purchase a 1950s Austin Westminster rally car in the hope that seasoned competitor Dave Mathewson can win a race

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A rare Reliant Scimitar GTC undergoes an expert recommission that leaves no bolt unturned, and a Honda CBR superbike from the 1980s needs a repair

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: Laurence Olivier narrates an account of the liberation of Paris and Brussels in 1944, events which to many signified the end of the war. However, as the German forces retreated, Poland fell victim to one of the most tragic events of the war, Documentary chronicling the Second World War

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The search for Josef Mengele, one of the most wanted Nazi war criminals, which was led by Holocaust survivor Simon Wiesenthal

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Ebrington Square in Londonderry, where highlights include a Celtic stone head that could be thousands of years old, rare Belleek vases bought for just a few pounds from a charity shop, and a drawing by Muhammad Ali. Plus, Mark Hill gets the chance to see a childs model Austin Pathfinder pedal car and Fiona gets a history lesson about Northern Irish motor racing when she finds out about the Crossle car company

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough presents a guide to the world of plants that grow in water, including a bubbling river in Brazil, where the plants have created their own atmosphere and a lake smothered in brightly-coloured flowers. Plus, plants that fight each other for growing space or use natural adhesives to prevent themselves being swept downstream, and a look at the methods used to evade or even attack animals
